jobs.description:

Sungarden Terrace Assisted Living & Memory Care
2045 Skyline Drive
Lemon Grove, CA 91945

Sungarden Terrace is a boutique Assisted Living and Memory Care community located in Lemon Grove, California. With over 25 years of experience serving the senior population, we are highly regarded for our commitment to quality care, strong leadership, and a supportive environment for both residents and staff.
We are seeking an experienced, hands-on Executive Director/Administrator to lead our team and continue driving excellence in operations, care, and community engagement.

Competitive base salary

jobs.description:
DEVELOPMENT ASSOCIATE – DONOR OPERATIONS & COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT Department:
Development
Reports To:
Chief Executive Officer
Status:
Part-Time, Non-Exempt
Compensation:
$27.81 per hour
Schedule:
25 hours per week

Position Summary United Way of Santa Cruz County is seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Development Associate – Donor Operations & Community Engagement to support the organization's fundraising, donor stewardship, grant development, and revenue management efforts.
Reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer, this position serves as the primary lead for donor operations, grants management, workplace campaigns, CRM administration, fundraising reporting, and gift processing. The position plays a critical role in securing and stewarding both unrestricted and restricted funding that advances United Way's mission and strategic priorities.
This position works closely with the Development Associate – Marketing & Community Engagement. While each role has designated areas of ownership, both positions collaborate on fundraising initiatives, sponsorship development, community engagement activities, and organizational events.

Essential Duties & ResponsibilitiesDonor Operations & CRM Management ·        Serve as primary administrator of the organization's donor database and CRM platform.
·        Process donations, sponsorships, pledges, recurring gifts, and other charitable contributions.
·        Maintain accurate donor, sponsor, volunteer, and stakeholder records.
·        Generate fundraising reports, dashboards, and donor analytics.
·        Manage donor acknowledgements and tax receipts.
·        Ensure data integrity and quality control across all fundraising systems.
·        Assist with donation reconciliation and financial reporting.
Grants Management ·        Research and identify grant opportunities from government, foundation, corporate, and community funding sources.
·        Prepare, write, and submit grant proposals, letters of inquiry, sponsorship requests, and funding applications.
·        Manage grant reporting requirements and compliance deadlines.
·        Coordinate with program and finance staff to collect outcomes, financial information, and supporting documentation.
·        Maintain grant calendars, records, agreements, and reporting schedules.
·        Assist with identifying both unrestricted and restricted funding opportunities.
Workplace Campaigns & Fundraising Operations ·        Coordinate workplace giving campaigns and employee engagement activities.
·        Serve as staff liaison to participating employers.
·        Track campaign performance and fundraising outcomes.
·        Support sponsorship tracking and stewardship activities.
·        Conduct donor and prospect research.
·        Support fundraising campaigns and annual giving efforts.
Community Engagement ·        Build and maintain positive relationships with donors, sponsors, volunteers, and community partners.
·        Represent United Way at meetings, events, presentations, and community gatherings as assigned.
·        Deliver presentations and speak on behalf of the organization when requested.

Shared Organizational ResponsibilitiesEvents & Fundraising ·        Plan, coordinate, and support all United Way fundraising and community events.
·        Assist with event logistics, sponsorship fulfillment, registration, volunteer coordination, donor engagement, and event execution.
·        Participate in sponsor cultivation and stewardship activities.
·        Support fundraising campaigns and revenue-generating initiatives.
·        Assist in identifying and securing unrestricted funding opportunities.
Team Collaboration ·        Work closely with the Development Associate – Marketing & Community Engagement to achieve fundraising and engagement goals.
·        Support interns and volunteers assigned to development projects.
·        Collaborate across United4Youth, United4Community, 211, and other organizational initiatives.
·        Assist other departments and organizational priorities as needed.
Organizational Culture United Way of Santa Cruz County operates in a highly collaborative environment. Staff members are expected to work across departments and initiatives, support organizational priorities beyond their primary responsibilities, and contribute to the success of fundraising efforts, community engagement activities, volunteer initiatives, and special events. Flexibility, teamwork, and a mission-first mindset are essential to success.

Knowledge, Skills & Qualifications ·        Associate's or Bachelor's degree preferred or equivalent experience.
·        Minimum two years of experience in fundraising, donor relations, grants management, nonprofit administration, or related field.
·        Experience managing CRM or donor database systems.
·        Strong written communication and grant writing skills.
·        Excellent organizational and project management abilities.
·        Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities.
·        Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite and cloud-based software.
·        Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
·        Strong interpersonal and presentation skills.
·        Bilingual English/Spanish preferred.

Work Schedule & Expectations ·        25 hours per week, generally Monday through Friday, approximately five hours per day.
·        Schedule may vary depending on fundraising campaigns, grant deadlines, community meetings, and organizational priorities.
·        Evening and weekend work will occasionally be required.
·        Local travel throughout Santa Cruz County is expected.
·        Attendance at fundraising events, community events, volunteer activities, and organizational functions is required.

jobs.title: Chief of Staff to CEO
jobs.description:
Replit is the agentic software creation platform that enables anyone to build applications using natural language. With millions of users worldwide, Replit is democratizing software development by removing traditional barriers to application creation.

About The Role

This is a rare opportunity to work directly with Replit's CEO and executive team as the company scales through one of the most consequential shifts in software development. You'll act as a force multiplier for the CEO — owning the hardest cross-functional problems, driving the highest-stakes decisions, and ensuring leadership operates with clarity and speed.

This is a high-leverage IC role, not a team-building role. You use AI, automation, and sharp judgment to scale yourself — not headcount. You're the person the CEO turns to when something is complex, cross-functional, and needs a single owner.

You Will

Act as a trusted advisor to the CEO and exec team — elevate insights, prep leadership for key decisions, and when needed serve as a stand-in
Proactively resolve problems before they reach the CEO — you see around corners and close gaps
Coordinate the CEOs network to accelerate go-to-market including in international markets like MENA where there is existing regional expertise and existing strong relationships
DRI decisions that span Product, Eng, Finance, and GTM — surface risks and disagreements early, drive alignment where org structure creates friction
Develop integrated views of product economics, market dynamics, and business performance that no single function owns
Use AI, automation, and agent workflows to scale your impact: analysis synthesis, scenario modeling, status aggregation, operational monitoring
Support the CEO's most important cross-functional initiatives — competitive positioning, pricing strategy, product economics, strategic planning — from scoping through decision

What This Role Is Not

Growth experimentation, quarterly reporting, analytics, revenue or financial reporting, or dashboards. Those belong to Data, RevOps, Growth Marketing, Strategic Finance, etc. This is also not the start of a team — it's a single high-leverage IC designed to scale through AI, not hiring.

What We Look For

7+ years in strategy, operations, or an analytical hybrid role at a high-growth tech company, with real time working alongside Product and Engineering
You build structure where none exists and drive decisions without formal authority
Credible across domains: Eng trusts your technical judgment, Finance and GTM trust your business rigor
Strong quantitative chops — economic models, data interrogation, signal vs. noise
Clear, direct communicator — executive-ready recommendations, not decks full of caveats
Thrives independently in ambiguity — high-impact IC, not a management track
Familiarity with technology ecosystems or business networks in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), or experience supporting international market expansion and strategic partnerships.

Nice to Haves

Hands-on experience with AI tools, agents, or automation to increase analytical and operational velocity — we'd love to hear specifics
Background in developer tools, infrastructure, or AI/ML product companies
Track record influencing executive decisions at $50M+ ARR companies
Values clarity and momentum over process for process's sake

How We'll Know It's Working

Cross-functional decisions happen faster and better — with clear, attributable impact
Stakeholders seek you out — your involvement signals quality, not overhead
Leadership has integrated visibility into business health and acts on it

This is a full-time role that can be held from our Foster City, CA office. The role has an in-office requirement of Monday, Wednesday, and Friday.

Competitive Salary & Equity

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Are you a decisive leader who thrives in high-growth environments and is eager to drive operational excellence at a dynamic, tech-forward, law firm?

Can you identify and fix inefficiencies in real-time, ensuring seamless operations across growth marketing, brandings, tech stacks, HR, and more?

Do you possess an unparalleled ability to integrate cross-functional teams, ensuring alignment and streamlined execution?

Are you prepared to take on the challenge of eliminating bottlenecks and accelerating growth alongside a fast-paced visionary leadership?

Final question:
When faced with a challenge, do you get excited or run away?

** IMPORTANT: TO APPLY **

If you have any questions, please email recruit@crisp.co only.

Please do not reach out through our website's contact information (telephone, email, or web chat) or via direct messaging on any social media platform.

To Apply: Submit your application by clicking the "Apply" button and applying on the Crisp Recruit page that opens.

**RECRUITERS DO NOT CONTACT**

At Levi & Korsinsky LLP, a nationally recognized class action law firm based in New York City, we have spent over two decades fighting for the rights of consumers and shareholders. Our powerhouse team of attorneys, admitted in over 40 courts nationwide, combines deep expertise with innovative litigation strategies to tackle some of the largest, most complex cases in the country.

We are relentless in holding corporations and their executives accountable for consumer protection violations, securing significant settlements and landmark legal precedents that protect individuals and shape industry behavior.

As the Director of Operations for Levi & Korsinsky LLP, you will play a pivotal role in propelling the firm’s growth trajectory in consumer related class and mass actions. Tasked with transforming strategic vision into operational reality, you will work closely alongside, and in many ways, act as the extension of, the Founder to take ownership of key initiatives, solve organizational challenges, streamline operations, and help drive execution across the business. Your role is crucial in aligning the firm's innovative practices with its overarching mission of empowering consumers and optimizing the firm’s high-stakes business approach.

This role is not stepping into a mature machine. The firm’s securities practice has
been around for 20+ years, you will be working on growing the consumer practice
and its associated verticals that sits alongside the securities practice. You will be part
of a team helping build stronger systems while driving immediate execution in a fast-moving, evolving environment.

What you’ll do:

Operational Execution:
Drive execution of strategies across the firm, ensuring alignment between strategy and day-to-day execution. Identify and address inefficiencies, strengthen workflows, and help ensure seamless operations.
HR and Talent Strategy:
Build and optimize the firm’s recruiting engine and people systems, including passive talent sourcing, screening rigor, onboarding, and performance accountability, helping strengthen the team infrastructure needed to support growth.
Marketing Oversight:
Provide strategic oversight across marketing efforts, ensuring campaigns are aligned, properly targeted, and executed effectively, while helping identify breakdowns in process, quality, and performance.
Systems, Technology, and AI:
Evaluate and implement tools, automation, and systems that improve efficiency, strengthen workflows, and support smarter, faster execution across the business.
Cross-Functional Leadership:
Coordinate execution across legal, operations, marketing, offshore teams, and external partners, creating clear alignment, stronger accountability, and more effective execution across a complex, globally distributed organization.
Problem Solving & Execution:
Take ownership of complex, ambiguous challenges, identify practical solutions, and drive decisions forward with sound judgment and decisive action.
Build and Scale in Motion:
Help strengthen systems, solve breakdowns, and build infrastructure while the business continues to grow, driving execution in a fast-moving environment where priorities shift quickly, complexity is constant, and maintaining momentum is critical.

What we’re looking for:

Operational Leadership:
Extensive experience leading and scaling operations in a high-growth, fast-moving, entrepreneurial environment; startup or growth-stage operating experience strongly preferred.
Operator Mindset:
Demonstrated ability to step into complex, evolving environments, bring structure to ambiguity, and drive execution with a high degree of ownership and sound judgment.
Strategic Insight:
Proven ability to identify patterns across functions, anticipate challenges, and develop scalable solutions that strengthen systems and improve organizational performance.
Financial and Operational Acumen:
Comfort using KPIs, data, and ROI thinking to evaluate tradeoffs, improve performance, and support sound operating decisions.
Change Leadership and Influence:
Ability to earn trust, navigate resistance, and drive change across strong personalities and cross-functional teams through credibility, judgment, and execution.
Recruitment Expertise:
Proven record in building and managing talent acquisition and retention systems for high-performing teams.
Global and Vendor Coordination:
Experience leading through offshore teams, external partners, or distributed operating environments, with strong instincts for coordination, accountability, and quality control.
AI and Automation Fluency:
Strong familiarity with AI, automation, and emerging tools, with the ability to leverage technology to improve efficiency and execution.
Problem-Solving Ability:
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to assess complex challenges, exercise sound judgment, and drive practical solutions under pressure.

Why you should work here:

Leadership Collaboration:
Direct engagement with the firm’s leadership offers opportunities for mentorship and impactful decision-making.
Innovative Environment:
Work within a pioneering firm that values innovation, technology, and cutting-edge legal strategies.
Global Influence:
Participate in high-visibility projects that make a tangible impact on clients and global legal practices.
Empowerment and Autonomy:
Enjoy a significant degree of autonomy in decision-making and strategy implementation, while contributing to the firm’s legacy.

This is a unique opportunity to join a forward-thinking firm where your contributions will drive meaningful change and promote the firm’s mission of legal excellence. If you are a strategic, results-driven leader ready to make an impact, we invite you to apply and become part of our innovative journey.

jobs.title: Director of Partnerships
jobs.description:
About Pagaya

Shape the Future of Finance

Pagaya is building a leading artificial intelligence network to help our partners grow their businesses and better serve their customers.

Pagaya is a global technology company making life-changing financial products and services available to more people nationwide, as it reshapes the financial services ecosystem. By using machine learning, a vast data network and a sophisticated AI-driven approach, Pagaya provides comprehensive consumer credit and residential real estate solutions for its partners, their customers, and investors. Its proprietary API and capital solutions integrate into its network of partners to deliver seamless user experiences and greater access to the mainstream economy. Pagaya has offices in New York and Tel Aviv. For more information, visit pagaya.com.

Let's create better outcomes together!

About The Role

The Director of Partnerships at Pagaya will lead and expand strategic relationships across personal loan originators, fintech platforms, and bank partners at an executive level. This role is responsible for shaping partner strategy, driving revenue and profitability, and executing key initiatives to accelerate growth across Pagaya’s personal loan network. The position will play a critical role in scaling partnerships, identifying new business opportunities, and delivering measurable impact across volume, revenue, margin, and strategic outcomes.

Responsibilities

Industry & Partner Expertise: Serve as a subject matter expert within Pagaya on the personal loans ecosystem, including fintech lenders, digital platforms, and bank partnerships. Maintain a deep understanding of market dynamics, competitive landscape, and partner business models.
Business Delivery: Own and deliver on core partner KPIs, including origination volume, revenue growth, profitability, partner performance, and execution of strategic initiatives.
Business, Product & Solution Development: Identify and drive new opportunities within existing and prospective partners. Lead commercial discussions, negotiate agreements, and collaborate cross-functionally (including Product, Data, and Risk teams) to design and deliver tailored solutions that meet partner needs and unlock growth.
Strategy Development: Develop and execute comprehensive partner strategies and account plans, incorporating partner insights, long-term vision, growth opportunities, risks, KPI targets, and relationship development roadmaps.
Key Relationship Development: Build and maintain strong executive-level relationships across fintechs, banks, and ecosystem partners. Establish trusted partnerships that enable long-term success and collaboration.
Partner Communication: Lead all partner communications, including business reviews, strategic discussions, escalations, and negotiations. Ensure clear, consistent messaging and alignment across all engagements.
Internal Stakeholder Management: Partner cross-functionally across internal teams- including Legal, Product, Capital Markets, Operations, Risk, and Analytics - to ensure seamless execution, alignment, and issue resolution.
Leadership Updates & Preparation - Provide regular updates to senior leadership on partner performance, risks, and opportunities. Prepare executives for key partner interactions and strategic discussions.

Requirements

10+ years of experience managing strategic B2B partnerships, client relationships, and/or business development, preferably within fintech, consumer lending, or financial services.
Strong experience in personal loans, unsecured consumer lending, fintech platforms, and/or bank partnerships.
Proven ability to develop and execute partner strategies that drive measurable business growth.
Demonstrated success in managing and growing executive-level relationships.
Experience identifying, negotiating, and closing new business opportunities within existing or new accounts.
Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills, with the ability to evaluate complex business opportunities and partner performance.
Experience working cross-functionally in fast-paced, technology-driven environments.
Ability to influence and align multiple stakeholders across internal and external teams.
Excellent communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts and drive alignment.
Strong financial acumen, including experience with P&L management (or capital markets), unit economics, and performance metrics.
Experience managing global or cross-cultural teams is a plus.

The pay ranges for New York-based hires are commensurate with candidate experience.

Pay ranges for candidates working in locations other than New York may differ based on the cost of labor in that location.

Compensation Range for New York Based Hires

$200,000—$240,000 USD

Our Team

Pagaya was founded in 2016 by seasoned research, finance, and technology entrepreneurs with our head quarters located in NYC and Tel Aviv.

We move fast and smart, identifying new opportunities and building end-to-end solutions from AI models and unique data sources. Every Pagaya team member is solving new and exciting challenges every day in a culture based on partnership, collaboration, and community.

Join a team of builders who are working every day to enable better outcomes for our partners and their customers.

Our Values

Continuously Learn- We challenge ourselves for the sake of getting better as individuals, as teams, and as an organization to deliver for our partners.
Debate and Commit- We respectfully and openly debate to strengthen our ideas and build shared conviction - once we decide, we go all in, together.
Dream Big and Act- We boldly tackle complex problems, pressure-test solutions in real-time, and adapt with speed and energy.
Advance Inclusion- We create a world where everyone can win, designing systems that better represent people and generate sustainable value for our employees, partners and investors.
Be Accountable Together- We proudly own our actions and our results, taking initiative to ensure our work gets over the finish line as a team.

More than just a job

We believe health, happiness, and productivity go hand-in-hand. That's why we're continually looking to enhance the ways we support you with benefits programs and perks that allow every Pagayan to do the best work of their life.

• Lead execution of SIF’s strategic plan and refine priorities as the organization grows.

• Senior leadership experience in nonprofit management, education, youth development, community development, financial services, philanthropy, or a related field.
• Demonstrated fundraising success.
• Passion for financial literacy, educational equity, and expanding opportunity for underserved students.
• Strong communication and public-speaking skills.

First-Year Priorities • Build trust with staff, Board, schools, donors, and community partners.
• Assess current programs, fundraising capacity, and operating model.
• Strengthen SIF’s donor pipeline and corporate partnership strategy.
• Increase visibility for SIF’s student impact and financial education mission.
• Create a practical growth plan for expanding reach while preserving program quality.

jobs.title: Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner
jobs.description:
What We Can Achieve Together

The Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner functions as a leader and conservation strategist for a large, complex business unit in a global organization that plays an integral leadership role in TNC’s Midwest Division. The Midwest Division encompasses Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio and Wisconsin—a region of Great Lakes, great rivers and immense global importance for conserving biodiversity, tackling climate change, and protecting lands and waters as outlined in TNC’s 2030 goals. In addition to representing Michigan and the Midwest, this position must also be versed in national and global TNC priorities.

This position will be a key leader in a global fundraising campaign and is responsible for producing measurable conservation results, while leading a large, multidisciplinary team of professionals across the state. They serve as the primary spokesperson for TNC in Michigan to internal and external audiences (including public and private donors, media, the business community, the Michigan Board of Trustees, community leaders and other partners, government agencies and officials and staff). They cultivate those audiences to support and promote TNC’s mission and vision.

The Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner sets objectives and manages the performance of a large and collaborative team with a focus on strategy development, fundraising and managing the Board of Trustees. They work together across teams as they cultivate and solicit high-level donors (private and public) to meet fundraising goals. Additionally, this role supports conservation programs and initiatives, organization visibility, policy and government relations, the management of staff and the financial health of the business unit.

The Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner also collaborates with peer Midwest Executive Directors and the Midwest Division Director (known as the Midwest Board of Partners) in helping shape the strategy, development and execution of cross-boundary efforts within the Midwest and globally, including the contribution of financial and human resources. Along with other members of the Midwest Board of Partners, the Michigan Executive Director supports the Midwest 2030 conservation goals, and sponsors one or more conservation strategy and functional area teams at the regional level. They help align their team’s work to secure and deploy sufficient resources and capacity to achieve the Midwest’s maximum contribution to TNC’s 2030 conservation goals.

The Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner creates a healthy work environment for all TNC staff in the state and supports coordinated staff development across the region. This position reports to the Midwest Division Director and works in partnership with the Midwest Board of Partners and the Michigan Board of Trustees.

Location:
Within Michigan, preference for the greater Lansing, Ann Arbor, Detroit, or Grand Rapids areas.

We’re Looking For You

Are you looking for work that you can believe in? At TNC, we strive to embody a philosophy of “Work That You Can Believe In” where you can feel like you are making a difference every day. We’re looking for someone with strong experience leading a diverse, multidisciplinary team.

The ideal candidate should have exceptional leadership skills, with a strong background in high-level fundraising. They must be a compelling storyteller and talented communicator who can successfully use these talents to fundraise, build consensus and speak about TNC’s mission, vision and work—from state, regional and global perspective. This role must be able to effectively engage a wide range of partners, while constantly building and engaging the volunteer Board of Trustees.

This position requires a person with deep self-confidence tempered by genuine humility. They must have an ambitious yet collaborative nature. They must be very high-energy, intelligent, abstract thinkers willing to take bold, calculated risks while balancing the need to make transparent decisions in a matrixed and sometimes ambiguous environment. They must have very high emotional intelligence and be compassionate but also able to make tough decisions while being equitable, fair and inclusive.

This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to the ongoing mission of conservation by directing and shaping the work of Michigan and the Midwest.

What You’ll Bring

Bachelor’s degree and minimum of 7 years of experience as a leader in the conservation arena, non-profit sector, advocacy, or related for-profit area.
Extensive management experience, including the ability to motivate, lead, set objectives and manage the performance of a large multidisciplinary team.
Proven experience in cultivating and soliciting high-level donors to reach fundraising goals, which is a priority for this position.
Written and verbal fluency in English.
Effective presentation and communication skills for diverse audiences, including high-level donors, the media, the business community, board members, outside partners, staff or equivalent that lead to desired outcomes.
Experience with strategic fiscal management, including long-term sustainability.

Desired Qualifications

Seven to 10 years of experience as a proven leader in the conservation arena, nonprofit sector, advocacy, or related for-profit area, including demonstrated experience producing results and meeting organizational, divisional or business unit goals.
Experience working in the U.S. and globally preferred. Demonstrated commitment to developing and practicing global literacy.
Outstanding leadership skills, including thought-leadership, visionary qualities and the ability to inspire others in a decentralized, geographically dispersed and highly matrixed organization.
Knowledge of conservation and conservation practices.
High energy, forward-thinking and creative individual with high ethical standards.
Proven strategic thinking skills, creative problem-solving, adept decision-making, adaptability and ability to collaborate and influence.
Demonstrated experience in public speaking on behalf of a global organization with the capability to present on a wide range of science-based and conservation topics desired.
A strong network of local, regional and national contacts that include leaders from philanthropic, business and communities relevant to our conservation work.

This position requires a valid driver's license and compliance with TNC’s Auto Safety Program
. Employees may not drive Conservancy-owned/leased vehicles, rental cars, or personal vehicles on behalf of the Conservancy if considered "high risk drivers." To learn more about the Auto Safety Program, visit the Auto Safety information page. Employment in this position will be contingent upon completion of a Vehicle Use Agreement, which may include a review of the prospective employee's motor vehicle record.

Salary Information

The starting pay range for a candidate selected for this position is generally within the range of $185,000 - $218,000. This range only applies to candidates whose country of employment is the USA. Where a successful candidate’s actual pay will fall within this range will be based on a variety of factors, including, for example, the candidate's location, qualifications, specific skills, and experience. Please note countries outside the USA would have a different pay range in the local currency based on the local labor market, and not tied to USA pay or ranges. Your geographic location will be confirmed during the recruitment.

Who We Are

The Nature Conservancy’s mission is to protect the lands and waters upon which all life depends. As a science-based organization, we create innovative, on-the-ground solutions to our world’s toughest challenges so that we can create a world in which people and nature thrive. We’re rooted in our mission and guided by our values, which include respect for all people, communities, and cultures. Whether it’s career development, flexible schedules, or a rewarding mission, there’s many reasons to love life inside TNC. Want a better insight to TNC? Check out our TNC Talent playlist on YouTube to hear stories from staff or visit Glassdoor.

One goal is to cultivate an inclusive work environment so that all our colleagues around the globe feel a sense of belonging and that their unique contributions to our mission are valued. In addition to the requirements in our job postings, we recognize that people come with talent and experiences outside of a job and consider each applicant’s unique experience. Please apply – we’d love to hear from you. To quote a popular saying at TNC, “you’ll join for the mission, and stay for the people.”

What We Bring

Since 1951, TNC has been doing work you can believe in. Through grassroots action, we have grown from a small non-profit into one of the most effective and wide-reaching environmental organizations in the world. Thanks to more than 1 million members, over 400 scientists, and the dedicated efforts of our diverse staff and partners, we impact conservation around the world!

TNC offers a competitive, comprehensive benefits package including health care benefits, flexible spending accounts, a 401(k) plan with an 8% employer match, parental leave, accrued paid time off, life insurance, disability coverage, employee assistance program, other life and work well-being benefits. Learn more about our Benefits and Perks here.

We’re proud to offer a flexible work environment that supports of the health and well-being of the people we employ.

Our recruiting process includes a rolling interview process to ensure we engage applicants in a timely manner. This means we may review applications in the order in which they are received. Once a strong candidate pool is identified, the role will be unposted. The timeline may vary depending on the expressed interest in the role, so we highly encourage candidates to apply as soon as possible.

The Nature Conservancy is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
Our commitment to equal employment opportunity includes the recognition that our conservation mission is best advanced by the leadership and contributions of people of all backgrounds, beliefs, and culture. Recruiting and mentoring staff to create an inclusive organization is a priority, and we encourage applicants from all cultures, races, colors, religions, sexes, national or regional origins, ages, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity, military, protected veteran status or other status protected by law.

The successful applicant must meet the requirements of The Nature Conservancy's background screening process.

Do you have military experience? Visit our U.S. Military Skills Translator to match your military experience with our current job openings!

TNC is committed to offering accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application process. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please send a note to applyhelp@tnc.org with Request for Accommodation in the subject line.

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Executive Director

Financial & Estate Literacy (It’s Your Money & Estate)

Website: itsyourmoneyandestate.org

Location:
Orange County, California (Hybrid)
Position Type:
Full-Time
Compensation:
$85,000 – $125,000 annually, commensurate with experience
Benefits:
Comprehensive benefits package

About Financial & Estate Literacy

Financial & Estate Literacy (It’s Your Money & Estate) is a mission-driven nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering individuals and families with the knowledge and resources needed to make informed financial and estate planning decisions.
Through educational programs, community workshops, and strategic partnerships, the organization works to increase financial confidence, promote responsible planning, philanthropic giving and help prevent financial exploitation through awareness and education.

Position Overview

The Executive Director serves as the chief executive leader of Financial & Estate Literacy and is responsible for advancing the organization’s mission through strategic leadership, effective governance partnership, program excellence, community engagement, and operational stewardship.
Working closely with the Board of Directors, the Executive Director will translate the organization’s mission and strategic priorities into measurable outcomes while ensuring strong governance practices, financial sustainability, operational effectiveness, and continued program growth.
The ideal candidate is a collaborative, mission-focused leader who can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution and build meaningful relationships with donors, volunteers, educators, professional advisors, community organizations, and other stakeholders.
This is an exciting opportunity for a nonprofit leader who is passionate about education, financial empowerment, and creating lasting community impact.

Key Responsibilities

Executive Leadership & Strategic Direction

• Provide visionary leadership and strategic direction to advance the organization’s mission and long-term goals.
• Partner with the Board of Directors to develop and implement strategic plans, priorities, and initiatives.
• Translate organizational objectives into actionable programs, measurable outcomes, and sustainable growth strategies.
• Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, innovation, and continuous improvement.
Board Governance & Partnership

• Serve as the primary liaison between the Board of Directors and organizational operations.
• Support the Board in fulfilling its governance, fiduciary, and strategic responsibilities by providing timely, accurate, and meaningful information.
• Collaborate with the Board Chair and committees on strategic planning, organizational priorities, risk management, and performance evaluation.
• Ensure compliance with nonprofit governance best practices, organizational bylaws, policies, and regulatory requirements.
• Support Board recruitment, development, engagement, and ongoing education.
• Maintain a strong partnership between governance leadership and operational management.
Program Leadership & Community Impact

• Provide strategic oversight of the organization’s educational initiatives, including It’s Your Money
and It’s Your Estate
programs.
• Ensure programs are impactful, mission-aligned, accessible, and responsive to community needs.
• Expand educational outreach through workshops, partnerships, and innovative programming.
• Support facilitators, volunteers, and community partners in delivering high-quality educational experiences.
Operations & Organizational Management

• Oversee daily operations and ensure effective systems, processes, and infrastructure.
• Develop and maintain policies, procedures, and organizational practices that support accountability and efficiency.
• Coordinate staff, contractors, volunteers, vendors, and strategic partners.
• Ensure compliance with nonprofit standards and operational requirements.
Financial Stewardship & Sustainability

• Provide leadership for budgeting, financial planning, and responsible resource allocation.
• Work with the Board Treasurer and financial advisors to monitor financial performance and sustainability.
• Ensure sound financial practices, transparency, and responsible stewardship of organizational resources.
• Identify opportunities to strengthen revenue streams and long-term financial stability.
Fundraising & Resource Development

• Develop and execute fundraising strategies in partnership with the Board and fundraising committees.
• Cultivate relationships with donors, sponsors, foundations, corporations, and community supporters.
• Lead donor engagement, sponsorship development, grant opportunities, and stewardship efforts.
• Communicate organizational impact and funding priorities effectively.
Community Engagement & External Relations

• Serve as the organization’s primary ambassador and spokesperson.
• Build and maintain partnerships with professional advisors, nonprofits, educational institutions, corporations, and community organizations.
• Represent Financial & Estate Literacy at events, educational forums, and community meetings.
• Increase awareness of financial literacy, estate planning education, and prevention of financial exploitation.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications

• Demonstrated executive leadership experience in a nonprofit, educational, community-based, or mission-driven organization.
• Experience working with a Board of Directors and understanding of nonprofit governance principles.
• Proven ability to develop strategy, manage operations, and lead organizational initiatives.
• Strong financial management and resource development experience.
• Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
• Ability to inspire and engage donors, volunteers, partners, and community stakeholders.
• Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively.
• Bachelor’s degree required.
Preferred Qualifications

• CFRE designation
• Experience leading educational programs, workshops, or community outreach initiatives.
• Experience with fundraising, grants, sponsorships, or nonprofit development.
• Knowledge of financial literacy, estate planning, elder empowerment, aging populations, or charitable giving education.
• Experience working with professional advisors, financial professionals, attorneys, or community organizations.
• Entrepreneurial m indset with the ability to identify opportunities for growth and innovation.

Work Environment & Benefits

Position Type:
Full-Time
Work Location:
Hybrid position based in Orange County, California, with flexibility for remote work and in-person meetings, community events, and organizational activities.
Compensation:
$85,000 – $125,000 annually, based on qualifications and experience.
Benefits Include:

• Competitive salary package
• Health and wellness benefits
• Paid time off
• Professional development opportunities
• Opportunity to lead a mission-driven organization making a meaningful community impact

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit a resume and letter of interest describing their leadership experience, nonprofit background, and connection to the mission of Financial & Estate Literacy to Patricia Collins
at patricia@itsyourmoneyandestate.org
.
Financial & Estate Literacy is committed to advancing education and empowerment by helping individuals and families build financial confidence, plan for the future, and protect themselves from financial exploitation.﻿

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
DIRECTOR OF DEVELOPMENT

The Lussier Community Education Center (LCEC) is a vibrant community center with a spirited history of strong programs, broad partnerships, and grassroots community organizing. Located on the grounds of Ezekiel Gillespie Middle School and Vel Phillips Memorial High School, the LCEC has a mission of building community, creating opportunities, and enriching education .  

The Director of Development
will lead the overall fund development efforts for the LCEC, building fund development capacity through a diverse array of strategies in support of a nearly two million dollar operating budget. The Director of Development will work closely with the Executive Director to design and implement the Center’s fund development strategies as well as guide the organization’s marketing and communication plan. The ideal candidate will have strong writing and communication skills, understand the power of intentional relationships, and approach the work through a community-centric fundraising lens. As a member of the LCEC Leadership Team, the Director of Development plays an important role in guiding the growth of the Center toward fulfillment of its mission in alignment with the Lussier Framework .

Strong candidates will be creative, organized, flexible, and have experiential knowledge of fund development in nonprofits. They will be able to manage a diverse set of hands-on responsibilities and projects, while supporting and connecting with an effective, independent staff team in a grassroots, collaborative, community setting. The Director of Development reports to the Executive Director.  

Hours:
Full-time, on-site at West-side Madison location, generally 9 am - 5 pm M-F with some later evening and weekend hours required, as well as occasional off-site meetings and events as needed.

Compensation:
$74,000/year

Benefits:
100% employer-paid premium for health insurance plus a health reimbursement arrangement; competitive paid time-off package that includes pay for vacation, personal days, sick leave, and holidays; dental insurance; short- and long-term disability insurance; life insurance; 403(b); and flexible spending account.  

RESPONSIBILITIES  

Fund Development Management

Develop, update, and evaluate the LCEC Fund Development Plan with the Executive Director and lead its execution.
Monitor and communicate progress towards the annual fundraising goal to the Executive Director and Board of Directors.
Develop and maintain the systems needed to support the execution of the LCEC Fund Development Plan including the master fund development calendar, application and reporting deadlines, etc.

Individual and Business Donor Development

Manage and grow individual donor support through intentional and continued relationship building and relational meetings.
Maintain and expand the base of donors, potential donors, local businesses, service and school groups, and faith based organizations supporting the LCEC and our programs.
Work with the Executive Director to maintain and grow the base of major/recurring donors.
Maintain and evaluate the organization’s donor database and communications platforms.
Process and acknowledge all donations: log checks, prepare deposits, generate tax receipts, and ensure thank yous are created in collaboration with the Executive Director, staff, Board, volunteers, and program participants.
Organize donor events (Harvest, Friends of Lussier breakfasts, etc.) to raise resources, build relationships, and create community.
Explore opportunities for planned giving, stock and securities, cryptocurrency, property/estate, in-kind, and all other revenue generation resources.  

Grant, Foundation, and Sponsorship Development and Management

Ensure completion of proposals and regular program reporting to major institutional funders ( City of Madison, Dane County, United Way, CACFP, Second Harvest Kids Cafe, etc. )  
Identify and pursue funding opportunities through grants, foundations, local, state, and federal government, and other revenue generating resources.
Write timely, accurate, and effective grant proposals and ensure timely reporting.
Seek out and lead the development of collaborative grant proposals with partner organizations.  
Work with staff to identify matches between LCEC programs and grant opportunities.
Coordinate with directors and managers to ensure the timely completion of grant requirements and reports.
Gather relevant LCEC program data and evaluations to improve current and inform future program opportunities.  

Marketing and Communications

In collaboration with the Marketing and Communications Coordinator create a strategy to tell the story and celebrate the LCEC community and ensure its execution.
Create an organizational Annual Report, as well as other informational materials including brochures and materials/ information about special campaigns, initiatives, or events.
Work with the Director of Youth Programs and the youth programs team to ensure that photos and stories are captured to effectively tell our story.
Build relationships with local media outlets and invite and engage in their coverage of the organization’s work.
Support LCEC staff in media and public speaking opportunities.

Play a key leadership role in the strategic development of the LCEC as a whole 

Support the intentional integration of the Lussier Framework into all aspects of the LCEC’s work.
Actively engage in the strategic and project-based work of the Leadership Team to collaborate with and respond to community and organizational trends and needs.
Work with other directors to lead center-wide staff meetings, monthly community dinners and other center-wide events, and annual staff evaluation and goal setting.
Supervises the Marketing and Communications Coordinator.

Participate fully in shared work of the LCEC, including neighborhood events, staff retreats/meetings, and other projects as they arise.  

The Director of Development will perform all other duties and/or tasks at the discretion of the Executive Director as necessary for the successful operation of the LCEC.  

SOUGHT PERSONAL QUALITIES & TRAITS

・
Storyteller ・
Intentional collaborator ・
Engaging listener ・
Community-centric ・
Embraces diversity  ・
Learner and leader ・
Dreamer ・
Persuasive writer ・
Justice oriented ・
Demonstrates humility       ・
Detail-oriented ・
Curious       

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE

Minimum of 3 years experience in non-profit fundraising, including donor relations, grant writing and reporting.
Proficiency with websites, social media platforms, MailChimp or similar email platforms & Bloomerang or similar donor database management.
Experience with annual campaign planning and implementation, as well as digital and print communications.

SOUGHT QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE 

Strong verbal and written communication skills
High level of proficiency with the Google Suite.
At least 5 years of non-profit work experience
Previous supervisory experience
Language abilities in Spanish, Hmong, and/or Arabic

TO APPLY 

Complete the online application or pick one up at the Lussier Community Education Center, 55 S Gammon Road. Submit a resume, cover letter, writing sample, and completed application via email to jobs@LCECmadison.org , in person or by mail.

Writing Sample (750-1000 words) must be an example written by the applicant, including but not limited to a grant proposal narrative, a fundraising letter, or a written publication.  

For best consideration, please apply by July 31st, 2026. LCEC is an EOE/AA employer.

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER

SPY is looking for a leader who can guide an established youth homelessness organization through its next stage of scale, sustainability, housing growth, public advocacy, and strategic planning. The next leader must have an unwavering commitment to championing SPY's mission and the young people it serves; the ability to adaptively support and develop SPY staff; effectively lead a robust fundraising, external relations, and finance management strategy; and the experience to oversee a complex $15M organization operating 24 hour programs.

ABOUT SAFE PLACE FOR YOUTH

Safe Place for Youth is a leading youth homelessness organization serving young people ages 12 to 25 who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness in Los Angeles. SPY’s mission is to prevent and end youth homelessness through supportive services, sustainable housing opportunities, community collaboration, and advocacy, with a vision of a socially just and equitable world where all young people are empowered to thrive in self-directed lives and have a safe place to call home.

SPY provides a continuum of youth-centered services that includes street outreach, a drop-in center, case management, education and employment, health and wellness, healing arts, community garden, pregnant and parenting support, shelter, social enterprise, family reunification, housing, and advocacy. In 2025, SPY served more than 1,200 youth, helped 315 young people move into housing, and connected hundreds more to mental health counseling, case management, workforce training, creative arts, street outreach, and other critical supports.

SPY has entered a new stage of organizational complexity and opportunity. The organization now operates 10 sites across the County, including multiple housing programs across shelter, transitional housing, and permanent housing, providing 224 housing resources on any given night. SPY also provides robust supportive services through a field-based model and out of our drop-in center in Venice, including basic needs resources, case management, mental health, substance use counseling, education and employment, pregnant and parenting, healing arts and more.        

The next leader will be called upon to sustain operational excellence across housing and services programs, actively lead a robust fundraising strategy, ensure strong financial oversight, continue SPY’s leadership in advocacy, maintain strong community collaboration, support strong recruitment, retention and development of staff, while keeping the dignity and wellbeing of youth at the center of all of SPY’s work.      

POSITION OVERVIEW

SPY seeks a Chief Executive Officer
to lead the organization into its next phase of impact, sustainability, and public leadership.

The next leader will inherit a respected, mission-driven organization with a strong community identity, a seasoned senior team, an engaged Board, a growing housing portfolio, a newly redeveloped drop-in center, a planned capital campaign, and an urgent opportunity to shape the future of youth homelessness policy and practice in Los Angeles. The role requires a leader who can hold multiple responsibilities at once: stewarding a complex nonprofit enterprise, strengthening internal systems, serving as an external advocate and fundraiser, leading through a new strategic plan, and ensuring that SPY remains deeply connected to the young people and communities it serves.

The successful candidate will be a values-driven, emotionally intelligent, operationally capable, and externally credible leader who can balance ambition with sustainability. They will bring the strategic judgment to decide where SPY should grow, where it should consolidate, where it should advocate, and where it should say no. They will be equally comfortable with youth, staff, donors, elected officials, public agency leaders, community partners, and the Board.
WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Strategic Leadership:
Develop and execute strategic plans in alignment with the organization’s mission, vision, and values. Lead the organization in identifying trends, funding environment, challenges, and future growth opportunities in the housing environment.
Advocacy and Policy Engagement:
Serve as an external champion, advocate, and coalition builder for SPY. Build and maintain strong relationships with elected officials, public agencies, funders, community stakeholders, peer organizations, housing partners, researchers, advocates, neighborhood leaders, and media. Advocate for youth-specific homelessness funding, policy, housing resources, and service models.
Program and Housing Leadership:
Oversee the continued development of SPY’s continuum of care, including outreach, drop-in services, case management, education and employment, health and wellness, healing arts, pregnant and parenting supports, advocacy, shelter, transitional housing, permanent housing, and housing navigation.
Fundraising and Resource Development:

Lead fundraising efforts to secure financial support from diverse sources, including grants, donations, and corporate giving, and other forms of sponsorships.

Cultivate, solicit, and steward relationships with donors, foundations, and corporate partners to expand and diversify the organization's funding base and ensure financial sustainability.

Design expansion and business planning for program expansion. Build partnerships in new areas, establishing relationship with funders and political and community leaders for expansion sites.

Financial Management and Enterprise Sustainability:
Oversee SPY’s financial health in partnership with the CFO, Board Treasurer, Finance Committee, and senior leadership. Ensure strong budgeting, forecasting, cash flow management, reserves planning, audit readiness, internal controls, contract compliance, and financial reporting.
Staff Leadership and Culture:
Provide effective leadership and management of staff, volunteers, and consultants that fosters a collaborative and inclusive work environment while still maintaining accountability.
Board Relations and Governance:
Work closely with the Board of Directors to ensure effective board operations, governance, accountability, and transparency. Provide regular updates, reports, and recommendations to the Board to facilitate informed decision-making and strategic planning.
Other duties as assigned to support the mission of the organization.

REQUIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE YOU’LL NEED:

At least 5 years of executive leadership experience
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience required; advanced degree or relevant professional training preferred
Significant senior leadership experience in a nonprofit, public agency, housing, social services, youth-serving, advocacy, or mission-driven organization.
Demonstrated commitment to racial equity, social justice, LGBTQ+ inclusion, trauma-informed practice, and youth voice.
Ability to embody SPY’s values of care, community, inclusivity, innovation, integrity, and sustainability.
Demonstrated ability to lead teams, manage complexity, and build organizational infrastructure.
Experience with fundraising, external relations, public funding, and     institutional partnerships.
Strong financial literacy and experience managing or overseeing budgets, audits, contracts, and organizational risk.
Demonstrated adaptive and situational leadership, and experience mentoring and developing teams. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Past success working with a Board of Directors with the ability to cultivate board member relationships

WORK ENVIRONMENT AND PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS:

Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

Hybrid work environment, such as shared office space and/or remote work, with meaningful engagement at SPY sites.

Physical effort/lifting, up to 15-20 pounds at times.

SPECIAL CONDITIONS:

Mandated Reporting Requirements:
The person holding this position is considered a ‘mandated reporter’ under the California Child Abuse and Neglect Reporting Act and is required to comply with the requirements as a condition of employment.

BENEFITS & PERKS:

Safe Place for Youth offers a competitive benefit package including comprehensive health coverage, 403b with agency contribution, and vacation/sick time. We also offer unique perks including weekly meditation/mindfulness and professional development opportunities that are tailored to your individual career growth. 

COMPENSATION:

Salary is $210,000.00 to $230,000.00, commiserate with experience.

EEO STATEMENT:

All aspects of employment including the decision to hire, promote, discipline, or discharge, will be based on merit, competence, performance, and business needs. Safe Place for Youth does not discriminate on the basis of any protected status under federal, state, or local law. 

Safe Place for Youth is an equal opportunity employer committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce. In addition, the organization will consider employment-qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of the Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring.

Please include references in your application.

jobs.title: Director of Development - The Southeast Pastoral Institute (SEPI)
jobs.description:
Posted: January 13, 2026

Location: Southeast United States (Remote/hybrid options available}

About Us

US Catholic Bishop's Southeast Regional Office for Hispanic Ministry, also known as SEPI, coordinates and supports Hispanic Ministry in the 30 Catholic dioceses of the Southeast United States (Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee). Through its three pillars of Evangelization, Formation and Communion, SEPI strives to facilitate the full participation of Hispanics/Latinos in the mission of the Catholic Church and society as authentic missionary disciples. SEPI was born in 1979 as a response to the need for Hispanic education as recommended by the II National Hispanic Pastoral Meeting. SEPl's mission and values are to form and accompany Catholic leaders and communities in the Southeast by fostering communion, formation, and evangelization, grounded in the lived faith of Hispanic/Latino communities and in service to the Church's mission today.

Position Overview

SEPI is seeking a Director of Development to lead fundraising initiatives, cultivate donor relationships, and oversee all aspects of securing gift resources for the organization. This position is crucial for advancing SEP l's mission by securing philanthropic support and building sustainable relationships with individuals, foundations, and corporations. This person works directly with the SEPI Executive Director and works closely with key leadership and staff to design and implement strategies that drive philanthropic growth and sustainability.

This opportunity is ideal for someone motivated by the challenge to start and grow an effective, comprehensive development program. Prior nonprofit fundraising experience is preferred, but it is not required for candidates who demonstrate strong relationship-building instincts, a passion for purpose driven work and the ability to clearly communicate the value of philanthropy.

Key Functions

Develop and execute a comprehensive annual development plan with achievable goals and objectives. This plan should include activities in each of the key areas of a comprehensive development effort: major gifts, annual giving, special events and estate gifts. This plan will need to include actions to build the infrastructure for effective development work: donor database, donor identification, gift reports.
Maintain and manage a portfolio of high net worth/high interest prospects across the SEPI region by identifying gift opportunities that match their interests and personally cultivating, soliciting, and thanking these donors.
Create and clearly articulate a philanthropic case for SEPI needs, goals and objectives.
Track development results and report to appropriate SEPI staff and boards.

Key Qualifications

2-3 years' experience in fund raising, development or related fields preferred but not required.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to inspire and engage diverse audiences.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
Self-motivated, goal-oriented, and able to work independently as well as part of a team.
Passion for SEPl's mission and commitment to the Catholic faith and Hispanic Ministry.
Competitive salary and benefits package.
Opportunity to work in a mission-driven organization.
Professional development and growth opportunities.
Collaborative and inclusive work environment.

Application Process

Interested candidates should submit a resume and cover letter outlining their interest and qualifications for the Director of Development position to Dr. Olga Lucia Villar, aymeus@sepi.us. Please put the Job Title in the Subject Line. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.

SEPI is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applications from all qualified individuals committed to advancing Hispanic Ministry in the Southeast United States.

jobs.description:
Job Description

SALARY: Negotiable Depending on Qualifications

Under general administrative direction this position is responsible for planning, directing, managing, and overseeing the activities and operations of the Economic Development Department, including small business development, infill development, redevelopment, tax increment financing zones, economic development corporations, and industrial districts.

Essential Functions/ Job Competencies/ Physical Requirements

ESSENTIAL DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

The intent of this job description is to provide a representative summary of the major duties and responsibilities performed by employees in this job. Employees may be requested to perform job-related tasks other than those specifically presented in this description.

Oversees all operational activities of the Economic Development Department.
Formulates and administers the planning and grant writing activities for the City of Laredo. Research available grant sources and submits applications to all potential funding sources to include private foundations, local trusts, and to state and federal agencies.
Assures grant management in accordance with all federal, state, and local requirements including requirements stipulated by private foundations and/or trusts.
Directs the research, collection, and analysis of trade related data from production of reports and publications on economic status.
Supervises, plans, schedules, and assigns work to subordinates; instructs and trains in correct methods and procedures; reviews and evaluates employee's performance.
Develops and supervises the implementation of an annual operating and marketing plan, in order to promote Laredo as a destination to local, state, regional, national, and international audiences.
Directs the development and preparation of the annual departmental budget and monitors expenditures to ensure compliance with budget limitations.
Develops collateral materials including brochures, newsletters, promotional documents, advertisements, direct mail pieces and planner's guides to increase market shares for the Economic Development industry in Laredo.
Develops economic updates, reports, and publications.
Assists potential investors.
Directs the planning and coordination of promotional activities for the City of Laredo.
Promotes development operation in the City of Laredo and other foreign countries such as Mexico, China, and any other country interested in development and investing in our community.
Participates in international trade related conferences and trade shows for the purpose of attracting business and industry to our region.
Serves as the international trade liaison with foreign organizations.
Assists potential investors by organizing meeting agendas and providing regional economic and/or demographic data/maps.
Serves as conduit between private sectors and City Council for recommendation of economic policy.
Directs the development and maintenance of data on City of Laredo home page on the internet.
Represents City of Laredo in local and international forums by participating in meetings, conferences and/or trade shows.
Attends City Council meetings and takes necessary action regarding Council agenda items.
Works with an Advisory Committee made up of industry representatives that will make recommendations to the department and the City Council on policy changes needed to reflect the industry needs.
Briefs and advises City management, Mayor, and City Council regarding economic development and redevelopment projects, small business, tax increment financing zones, economic development corporations, and industrial districts in person and through written reports.
Participates in the development and administration of the budget for the tax increment zones, economic development corporations, and industrial districts.
Collaborates the City Manager, the Mayor, and city Council to define and articulate economic development visions, goals, strategies, and policies to formulate and implement strategic plans appropriate to the needs of the city and its citizens.
Educates economic development clients on City processes and helps facilitate the development process while working with City staff to balance the needs of economic development, permitting processes and City standards.
Develops and manages programs to assist new and expanding businesses in identifying resources and to provide financial incentive to encourage companies to locate in priority areas.
Develops and negotiates agreements between the City, private developers, and other governmental entities for economic development projects.
Will be required to drive a City vehicle for City business use.
Performs other duties as assigned.

Knowledge Of

Knowledge of public and business administration as it relates to functions, needs, policies, laws, ordinances, and regulations governing the City.

jobs.title: Director of Development, Alpha - $400,000/year USD
jobs.description:
Families at Alpha Miami rely on trusted advisors across every aspect of their lives. You will become the one they've been seeking in their child's education.

When uncertainty arises, when intuition signals something isn't quite right, or when a meaningful moment deserves to be shared with someone who truly understands—you'll be the person they reach out to. Your depth of knowledge about each family will enable you to foresee their needs before articulation becomes necessary, and you'll possess both the discernment and composure to address whatever they bring to you with confidentiality, genuine care, and seamless professionalism. Over time, your role will transcend being merely part of their child's educational institution. You'll become woven into the very fabric of how they navigate this chapter of family life.

The private school landscape in Miami is compact, deeply interconnected, and intolerant of errors. What happens at Alpha doesn't remain contained—it circulates through the networks that hold influence: residential buildings, exclusive clubs, yachts, charity galas, and the WhatsApp channels that determine where the next wave of families will apply. You'll grasp this ecosystem intuitively, drawing on a career spent in environments where reputation serves as the sole form of capital and where a single mistake can overshadow years of excellence. Your mission is to cultivate a community that families regard as precious, where membership feels natural and referrals emerge organically from the quality of the experience itself.

Miami's character is inherently international. Your work will involve Latin American families, global executives, households from finance and technology who have recently relocated to Miami, and internationally mobile families whose perspectives on education—and institutions more broadly—have been formed by experiences well beyond conventional American private schooling. Many navigate between Miami, São Paulo, Bogotá, Madrid, and New York with the same ease others show crossing neighborhoods, and they will evaluate Alpha against alternatives across the globe. The ideal candidate will be equally adept at managing cross-cultural family dynamics—and the expectation of sophisticated, fluid interpersonal relationships—as they are at navigating the more recognizable dimensions of Miami's private school community.

Alpha operates outside traditional educational frameworks. Students complete core academic requirements in two hours daily through AI-powered applications, dedicating remaining time to public speaking, critical thinking, and applied real-world projects. No conventional lectures. No unnecessary assignments. Performance outcomes in the top 1% nationally.

The families selecting Alpha have made a deliberate choice—and in a city where educational decisions carry social weight and everyone holds strong opinions, they will face constant questions. Your role is to ensure they never struggle for a response. If sustaining that conviction through years of sophisticated, internationally-informed scrutiny feels overwhelming, this position isn't suitable. If it represents the most compelling professional conversation available to you, continue reading.

What You Will Be Doing

Shape the cultural identity of the school community—establishing the tone, rituals, and implicit standards that foster genuine belonging—ensuring Alpha becomes a place families take pride in, not merely an institution they selected
Manage the complete parent relationship lifecycle—from initial contact through enrollment, continued engagement, and lasting advocacy—allowing faculty to concentrate on students while families maintain a reliable point of trust
Detect early signals when something feels misaligned, and address it with the discretion and judgment that preserves confidence—including the more difficult determination of when a family may not be an appropriate match
Maintain visibility in the spaces families occupy: morning arrival, afternoon departure, evening gatherings, and the spontaneous conversations that carry the most significance
Create a community that both domestic and international families regard as worthy of protection—and where word-of-mouth recommendations arise naturally from the caliber of experience provided
Communicate Alpha's narrative persuasively—in individual discussions, at formal events, and within the circles of discerning, globally-oriented families where the school's standing is genuinely established

What You Won’t Be Doing

Operating from behind a desk—your physical presence within the community defines this position
Maintaining conventional 9–5 schedules—early mornings, late afternoons, and evening commitments are integral to the role, not occasional requirements
Delegating challenging parent conversations to faculty or administrators—you assume complete ownership of these interactions
Relying on administrative infrastructure, established procedures, or a fully staffed team—you function with substantial autonomy and develop what's necessary
Approaching family relationships as accounts requiring management—this role rewards authentic connection rather than procedural adherence

Director Of Development Key Responsibilities

Serve as the primary trusted relationship at the heart of every family's journey at Alpha's Miami campus, and as the architect of its community culture. Maintain their confidence through difficult moments, establish the traditions and atmosphere that make belonging feel meaningful, and create the environment where satisfied families naturally become active advocates within the networks that influence Miami's private school ecosystem.

Basic Requirements

5+ years successfully earning and maintaining the confidence of high-net-worth or ultra-high-net-worth families in a relationship-intensive, high-touch capacity
Demonstrated success guiding sophisticated clients through consequential decisions—with measurable outcomes you can articulate: conversion rates, retention figures, referral generation
The discretion and poise necessary to navigate sensitive, confidential matters without creating lasting complications
Proven experience managing and developing personnel—providing coaching, maintaining elevated standards, and executing difficult staffing decisions—beyond simply managing relationships or client accounts
Inherent cross-cultural competence across both globally mobile families and established Miami households
AI-native orientation—you employ AI tools with fluency, understand their capabilities and constraints, and proactively identify superior solutions, even when you're not creating them independently
Miami-based, available for full-time on-site engagement, and adaptable to non-standard working hours
Legal authorization to work in the United States without requiring sponsorship

Nice-to-have Requirements

Background as a head of school, principal, superintendent, or in an equivalent educational leadership position
Professional-level fluency in Spanish or Portuguese
Personal or professional exposure to independent or non-traditional educational models
Pre-existing relationships within Miami's private school parent communities
Experience working with internationally mobile, Latin American, or globally connected family populations
A personal network extending through the neighborhoods, social clubs, and institutions where Alpha's families reside and make decisions

About Alpha

Join the team building school around student growth

Alpha reimagines the school day around faster learning, stronger mentorship, and more time for students to build real-world skills.

Roles with real student impact:
Alpha hires teachers (guides), operators, admissions experts, school & curriculum leaders, and remote team members.

Education work that pays like it matters:
Alpha offers six-figure roles for people ready to help build a radically better school experience.

A culture built for builders:
Alpha is fast-moving, ambitious, and student focused. It’s for people who want to improve the system, not maintain it.

Alpha is where you can do the most meaningful work of your career.

There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. Hit the Apply button if you found this interesting and want to learn more. We look forward to meeting you!

Working with us

This is a full-time (40 hours per week), long-term position. The position is immediately available and requires entering into an independent contractor agreement with Crossover as a Contractor of Record. The compensation level for this role is $200 USD/hour, which equates to $400,000 USD/year assuming 40 hours per week and 50 weeks per year. The payment period is weekly. Consult www.crossover.com/help-and-faqs for more details on this topic.
