jobs.title: DIRECTOR OF DEVELOPMENT, WEST REGION
jobs.description:
Director of Development, West Region

Share

Full Time
New Orleans, LA
Posted 2 months ago

Website The National World War II Museum

The National WWII Museum is currently seeking a Director of Development for the West Region. The Director of Development for the West Region reports to the Vice President of Institutional Advancement and is responsible for securing financial resources to support the Museum’s campaign priorities in the areas of Education and Programs; Collections, Exhibits, and Campus; and Endowment and Planned Giving. The Director will build relationships with individual, corporate, and foundation donors and prospects in the West Region. The West Region is defined as California, Oregon, Washington, and Arizona.

The Museum’s fundraising program is national in scope, and Development Directors are assigned to regional responsibilities. The Director will join a major-gift-focused development team that includes Directors of Development for the following regions: East/MidAtlantic, South/Central, and Louisiana/Texas, as well as a Director of Planned Giving and a Director of Corporate and Foundations. In addition to reporting directly to the Vice President of Institutional Advancement, Directors of Development also report secondarily to the Assistant Vice President of Institutional Advancement, who oversees prospect and pipeline management.

Requirements

Identify, cultivate, and solicit leadership and major-gift prospects; recommend and implement strategies for cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, and engagement for prospects; work with executive and Museum leadership and board members as appropriate; and meet with prospects both in person and virtually, with regular travel throughout the assigned region.
Contribute to the development of capital, programmatic, and endowment fundraising strategies to increase giving, with an emphasis on expanding the individual major-gift effort.
Manage a portfolio of approximately 150 prospects through all fundraising cycles – identification, qualification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship – meeting monthly agreed-upon donor contact and proposal submission goals, tracking activity and contact, and maintaining updates in the Museum’s CRM, Raiser’s Edge.
Develop and implement solicitation plans for each prospect; craft compelling, donor-centric proposals to support them.
Coordinate stewardship and acknowledgment activities, ensuring regular, systematic contact with prospects across all communication channels.
Assist Museum administrators in identifying institutional funding needs and staying up to date on institutional priorities, programs, personnel, and events.
Serve as the principal liaison to major gift prospects for the Museum, developing and maintaining a collaborative work environment with Museum departments to ensure a seamless donor journey and engagement.
Ability to plan and schedule your travel when visiting major-gift prospects in your region.

Qualifications And Attributes

Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree and CFRE certification are a plus.
5+ years of direct fundraising experience – with a clear understanding of annual, major, capital, and deferred gifts – and a demonstrable track record of securing six- and seven-figure gifts.
Experience in higher education, healthcare, or large arts/cultural organizations; demonstrable interest in lifelong learning.
Mastery of the skills, principles, practices, and techniques associated with implementing and successfully executing major-gift work on a national scale.
Highly developed interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills.
Working knowledge of Blackbaud Raiser’s Edge.
Proactive strategic thinker with strong organizational skills and the ability to work both independently and as a team member.
Flexible, adaptable, and proactive team player, comfortable with ambiguity and able to navigate sophisticated donor relationships.
Ability to manage work responsibilities while building donor relationships that require regular travel, evening and weekend meetings, and time commitments.

Work Environment

This position can be based in New Orleans or on the West Coast. If West Coast-based, the position requires regional travel and quarterly travel to New Orleans. If New Orleans-based, travel to the West region is required twice monthly. This is a full-time position operating on Central Time business hours. Standard working hours are Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. The employee must maintain a reliable internet connection and a professional home workspace suitable for remote work.

In addition to offering competitive wages, the Museum’s benefits package includes:

Medical insurance – 2 plan options; Museum pays 75% of premium
Dental and vision insurance
Flexible spending account
401(k) – Museum matches 50% of employee contribution up to 6%; employer contribution full vested after 3 years of employment
Life insurance and AD&D – $15,000 policy employer paid; additional life and AD&D available
Long term disability insurance
Paid vacation and sick leave, 10 paid holidays per year
Free parking
Tuition assistance and professional development
Employee assistance program

The National WWII Museum is an equal opportunity employer and seeks diversity in its workforce. We are dedicated to a policy of non-discrimination in employment on any basis including race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. Consistent with the Americans with Disabilities Act, applicants may request accommodations needed to participate in the application process.

To apply for this job please visit recruiting.paylocity.com.

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Director of Operations

Location:
On-Site

Compensation:
$130,000 Base Salary + Performance Incentive (On-Target Earnings $200,000+)
Industry:
Specialty Manufacturing | Off-Road Equipment

About the Opportunity
We are seeking a mission-driven Director of Operations to lead and scale a growing manufacturing business specializing in high-performance off-road equipment. This is a hands-on leadership role responsible for building high-performing teams, creating operational discipline, and driving execution across manufacturing, production, supply chain, quality, and distribution.

This opportunity is ideal for a military veteran who understands how to build teams, establish accountability, develop leaders, and execute under pressure. While manufacturing experience is important, we place an even greater emphasis on leadership, ownership, and the ability to create a culture built around performance and continuous improvement.
If you've successfully led people through complex environments, enjoy solving operational challenges, and want to leave an organization significantly better than you found it, we'd like to speak with you.

What You'll Do

Operational Leadership
Lead all day-to-day manufacturing and operational activities.
Drive productivity, efficiency, safety, and quality across the operation.
Develop scalable systems and standard operating procedures.
Identify operational bottlenecks and implement sustainable solutions.
Establish KPIs and accountability throughout the organization.

Team Development
Recruit, develop, and mentor supervisors and frontline leaders.
Build a culture centered on accountability, ownership, and continuous improvement.
Coach leaders through performance management and professional development.
Create succession plans and develop future operational leaders.
Foster strong communication between production, engineering, purchasing, and executive leadership.

Manufacturing Excellence
Improve throughput, reduce waste, and optimize production scheduling.
Partner with engineering to improve manufacturability and product quality.
Lead Lean Manufacturing and continuous improvement initiatives.
Ensure inventory, materials, and production resources are effectively managed.
Drive on-time delivery while maintaining quality standards.

Financial & Strategic Ownership
Own operational budgets and resource planning.
Improve gross margins through operational efficiencies.
Evaluate capital equipment and process improvement opportunities.
Support strategic growth initiatives and capacity planning.
Present operational performance metrics and improvement plans to executive leadership.

Safety & Compliance
Build and reinforce a world-class safety culture.
Ensure compliance with OSHA and all applicable manufacturing regulations.
Maintain quality systems and operational documentation.

What We're Looking For

The ideal candidate is a builder—not simply a manager.

You know how to earn trust, develop people, create structure where none exists, and hold teams accountable while maintaining high morale. You thrive in environments that require decisive leadership, operational discipline, and continuous improvement.
Military leadership experience is strongly preferred, particularly candidates who have led teams in demanding, high-performance environments.

Qualifications
Military leadership experience strongly preferred.
8+ years of operations leadership experience in manufacturing, industrial, or related environments.
Experience leading managers, supervisors, and cross-functional teams.
Demonstrated success improving operational performance and building scalable systems.
Strong understanding of manufacturing operations, production planning, quality, and supply chain.

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Major Areas of Focus

The Director of Operations (DOO) has P&L responsibility for their business unit. They are directly responsible for all sales in their region and the employees, equipment, real estate, and general infrastructure required for the fulfillment of those sales. The DOO works closely with the Director of Home Office and Director of Business Development.

The DOO has the most employees under his/her care and has a great responsibility to ensure all are developed according to ECF Culture. Our organizational plan places the DOO only four levels away from the front line. He knows the appearance of his front-line, their names, ranks, strengths, weaknesses, goals, and dreams. He is a steward of the company's mission statement.

The DOO is responsible for the development and implementation of the operating budget. This includes the sales budget. The DOO oversees Account Executives and supports the sales process by promoting the ECF Brand, attending sales presentations, and negotiating agreements.

Accountable for the management of a division of the company that spans multiple geographic markets
Is a sales, operations, and safety leader
Is a champion of ECF Culture, and gives thoughtful attention and care to front-line workers
Works closely with Executive Management
Reports to the President

Essential duties and responsibilities include the following. Other duties may also be assigned.

Safety

Always sets the example in matters of safety
Responsible for implementation of all training schedules and initiatives as set by the Corporate Safety Committee
Directly responsible for any lost time accidents that occur within the division coverage area
Directly responsible for safety infractions committed by division personnel
Maintains self-certifications including OSHA 10, ASM (if division spans snow markets), and appropriate pesticide licenses

Training & Development

Responsible for implementation of weekly Crew Leader muster training as scheduled by the Home Office
Ensures that operations managers and supervisors are training crew leaders and crew members in a meaningful way including training on soft skills, technical skills, and safety.

Leadership

Cares about people, and helps them to grow and achieve their personal goals
Maintains a visible and strong presence at the Service Centers, satellites of the division, and on the job sites
Is a professional steward of company initiatives, policies, and priorities as set forth by ownership
Always preserves and promotes the Chain of Command
Ensures that all subordinates are closely adhering to company policies
Participates in and drives the ECF Egress Standard and the ECF Ingress Standard
Develops and mentors personnel by ensuring that APR and MPR performance review programs are on schedule and effectively implemented

Financial

Builds draft annual operating budgets with the company Controller and submits them for approval
Builds monthly live budgets with the company Controller and submits them for approval
Manages the division’s P&L, and is directly responsible for all cost of goods sold expenses.
Manages AR for the division
Responsible for purchasing goods, services, and materials in line with job budget; a supervisor must approve overages
Manages all inventory of the division
Completes all appropriate internal documentation related to purchasing procedures
Manages and drives job budget of hours and materials, ensures margin budget is met

Facilities

Is directly responsible for all facilities and fleet belonging to the division
Ensures that the facilities and fleet are clean and orderly; within ECF 5S standards
Manages and directs fleet maintenance and mechanical service operation

Client Management

Coordinates with team members, clients, and operations leaders to ensure customer satisfaction is at its highest possible level, before, during, and after the delivery of a completed project scope
Manages customer service-related activities
Works closely with the Director of Business Development to ensure that handoffs of new clients are seamless
Is directly responsible for local client retention from a fulfillment perspective

Human Resources

Follows company policies and ensures that team members are obeying all safety, quality, and excellence policies and guidelines
Drives recruiting, the hiring process, and development of team members
Oversees all hiring managers (Production Managers) and ensures their departments are always staffed
Manages job hours and ensures that adequate staff is available to meet the needs of assigned projects
Follows and administers appropriate disciplinary protocols that are applicable to subordinates
Ensures that all branding and uniform compliance are always in order
Works closely with corporate human resources

Sales

Ensure all company policies and systems are in full use
Ensures that production managers are focused on providing proposals to clients for any assigned previously sold and established service contracts
Conduct or participate in bidding & takeoff processes, for any projects assigned by the sales team or the corporate office
Seek and obtain referrals for additional work opportunities

Quality Control

Ensure every project meets quality standards upon completion
Execute a Quality Control Program on assigned projects
Obtain, review and act on quality control inspection results
Initiates and manages annual customer experience surveys

Image Control

Ensure assigned fleet, personnel uniforms, grooming, facility cleanliness, and job sites meet our corporate standards of professional image
Conduct etiquette, behavior, and professional standards training to all subordinates
Ensure that all personnel are observing safety standards during working hours, at job sites, in public places, in transit, and at branch facilities, and such standards are clearly seen by the general public

Language Skills

While performing duties, employees are regularly required to use written and oral communication skills; may read and interpret data, information, and documents; observe and respond to people and situations; learn and apply new information or skills; work under deadlines; and interact with others encountered in the course of work. Proficiency in English is required, the ability to speak fluent Spanish is highly beneficial.

Physical Demands

The employee frequently is required to stand, walk and sit. The employee is occasionally required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel; reach with hands and arms; climb or balance; and stoop, kneel, or crouch. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 60 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Long working hours are required for emergency, urgent response or winter weather storm management operations.

Work Environment

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is occasionally exposed to wet and/or humid conditions, moving mechanical parts, fumes or airborne particles, toxic or caustic chemicals, and outside weather conditions. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate.

Travel & Development

Travel throughout the market served by the Allentown Service Center, from time to time for purposes of training, the employee may be required to travel to other offices or training centers operated by East Coast Facilities, Inc.

Security

The employee may not use illicit drugs during their employment with ECF. ECF operates in several high-security environments which require individuals who are able to pass security background checks. The employee is required to pass security background checks that meet our underwriting standards. The employee is required to have a valid driver's license and driving record that meets our underwriting standards. We reserve the right to reject an applicant or terminate an employee whose criminal or driving record fails to meet our underwriting criteria.

They are not intended to be a comprehensive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required of employees in this job family series. Other duties may be assigned as needed.

jobs.title: Program Director - Data Strategy, Policy & Advocacy
jobs.description:
At ACT, Your Work Makes a Difference

Education has power – a power that changes lives forever. It creates opportunities that lift up individuals, their families, and sparks societal change that echoes through generations to come. From our grassroots we have fought the good fight for equity in education, and we remain devoted to helping anyone who struggles to access that power. This is what matters to us and we must do better -- we’ve never been more sure of our purpose.

ACT team members are part of an organization dedicated to a mission that has never been more important: Helping people achieve education and workplace success. Advancing that mission within our organization, by helping our team members achieve education and workplace success of their own, is core to our values. ACT values a diverse workplace and is committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable environment in which every team member has an opportunity to grow.

We want our team members to have the well-being and confidence they need to do their best work, in an environment where both they and ACT thrive. To support this, our total rewards include company paid life insurance, medical, dental, vision, flexible health and dependent spending accounts, 401(k) retirement savings with company match, paid holidays, paid time off, and so much more. You can find a comprehensive list of our benefits here.

We are seeking a Program Director, Data Strategy, Policy & Advocacy
to help us fulfill that mission. As education systems face growing demands for data-informed decision-making, from assessment design to college readiness benchmarks, ACT is building its internal capacity to generate, synthesize, and communicate rigorous analyses that advance equitable student outcomes.

The Program Director, Data Strategy, Policy & Advocacy
will play a central role in building and sustaining this capacity. This is an opportunity for a mission-driven data professional who thrives at the intersection of analytics, policy research, and strategic communication – someone who can translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for policymakers, partners, and the public.

The Program Director, Data Strategy, Policy & Advocacy will lead high-impact work across three areas:

Data storytelling and thought leadership to support ACT’s influence in the field
Policy research and analysis to inform ACT’s external positions and priorities
Partner Engagement and evidence building to grow ACT’s evidence base and assess the impact of ACT programs and partnerships

The finalist will be hired at the salary and level commensurate with their qualifications. For this position, we anticipate offering an annual salary in the range of $115,000 to $130,000.
ACT manages salaries within the range based on relevant factors including, skills, experience, and internal equity. The position is incentive eligible.

Location:
This is a remote position, based in the United States.

Visa:
Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. without the need for visa sponsorship.

What You Will Be Working On

Data Storytelling and Thought Leadership: Embed data-driven decision-making across ACT’s Government and Public Relations team and serve as a strategic thought partner to ACT leadership and customers.
Key responsibilities include:
Support leadership in using data to inform strategic planning and resource allocation. Design and maintain data tools, dashboards, presentations, and visualizations that make key metrics accessible and actionable for internal and external stakeholders
Proactively identify research questions and analytic opportunities aligned with ACT’s policy priorities
Create feedback loops between research findings, program design, and partnership strategy
Provide capacity-building support to colleagues: training staff on data tools, analytic frameworks, and evidence-based practices
Maintain a pulse on external data trends, policy developments, and methodological advances relevant to ACT’s work

Policy Research & Analysis: Generate rigorous, policy-relevant analyses that inform ACT’s positions and contribute to national conversations about education equity, assessment, and college and career readiness.
Key responsibilities include:
Provide strategic input to inform quantitative and mixed-methods research studies to answer high-priority policy questions
Conduct literature reviews and synthesize research on education policy topics, presenting findings in actionable formats for policy and program staff
Analyze large-scale education datasets (assessment results, demographic trends, program outcomes) to identify patterns and inform strategic recommendations
Develop and operationalize metrics and frameworks for evaluating policy-relevant outcomes
Incorporate equity analysis to understand disparities in outcomes across student populations
Identify relevant external data sources (federal, state, institutional) and build efficient workflows for data acquisition and management
Translate complex analyses into clear written products (briefs, memos, reports) for non-technical audiences including policymakers and funders

Partner Engagement & Evidence Building: Design and implement data-sharing partnerships that build an evidence base for ACT programs, partnerships, and policy initiatives.
Key responsibilities include:
Systematize data-sharing agreements and data collection workflows with external partners (districts, states, institutions)
Support the development of reporting templates that can scale across partner engagements
Analyze variation in stakeholder contexts and identify conditions associated with strong outcomes
Support the development of clear metrics that define and operationalize success for ACT programs and partnerships
Generate impact reports that translate research findings into actionable insights for internal teams and external stakeholders

This could be the job for you if you have (minimum requirements):

At least 7 years of experience using data and analysis at the district or state level to inform decision-making in education, government, nonprofit, or social impact contexts, including demonstrated experience working with education data, advising senior leaders and shaping organizational strategy
Strong quantitative analysis skills
Familiarity with educational research designs and methods
Experience producing external-facing policy reports, case studies, or thought leadership
Experience building dashboards and other data visualizations, e.g. Tableau, MS Power BI or similar software
Experience designing surveys, analyzing assessment data, or evaluating programs or policies
Ability to translate complex data and analyses into clear written reports and presentations for non-technical audiences
Proven project management skills and ability to independently lead and manage multiple complex workstreams simultaneously, with minimal oversight
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Demonstrated commitment to improving educational outcomes and advancing equity
Ability to Travel approximately 10-15%
Bachelor's degree in education policy, public policy, economics, statistics, data science, or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

It’s a Plus If You Have

Experience at an SEA, large LEA, or federal agency working with K–12 or postsecondary education data, including state assessment or accountability data
Experience partnering with school districts, state education agencies, or higher education institutions
Strong stakeholder management skills and experience working cross-functionally
Curiosity, learning orientation, and comfort working in a mission-driven, fast-paced environment
Master’s degree in education policy, public policy, economics, statistics, data science, or a related field

About ACT

When ACT was founded in 1959, it disrupted the assessment industry with a new approach to helping students better understand their readiness so they could take steps to improve it. By leveraging our expertise and authority in assessment and research, we will again disrupt the industry—helping more people learn, better measure their progress, and improve their navigation through life’s transitions.

More than ever before, ACT is fulfilling its mission of helping people achieve education and workplace success. We’re doing it by pushing the boundaries of learning innovation through the work of our people, who we call team members because we’re all in this together.

We know transformation does not come without challenge. That’s why ACT invests in a variety of experiences for team members to strengthen their connections, explore ideas, learn from customers, and celebrate success.

Learn more about working at ACT at act.org!

ACT is an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. ACT participates in E-verify. ACT's online position announcements are intended only to provide general descriptions of employment opportunities; none of the information provided for any position should be interpreted as a commitment by ACT to specific terms and conditions for employment.

Completion of the ACT National Career Readiness Certificate is recommended. Find a testing site near you to register for the WorkKeys assessments. Finalists for this position will be subject to a criminal background check as a condition of employment.

If you have received an ACT employment-related communication from an email address that is not affiliated with @act.org and/or that requests personal or financial information, please do not reply. Instead, reach out to us at careers@act.org with the details. ACT's recruitment team appreciates your interest in working with us to transform college and career readiness pathways for all learners and wishes you the best of luck in your career journey.**

jobs.title: Director of Partnerships and Community Outreach, Alpha - $200,000/year USD
jobs.description:
On-site (multiple locations listed in Requirements)
W2 Employee
$200K annually

Families who select Alpha already rely on trusted advisors across every dimension of their lives. This role fills the one advisory relationship they've been lacking.

You will become the person they reach out to when uncertainty arises, when something doesn't feel right, or when they want to share a milestone they know you will appreciate. You'll develop familiarity with each family that allows you to foresee their needs before they articulate them—and you'll possess the judgment and composure to address whatever they bring with discretion, warmth, and ease. Over time, you won't simply represent their child's school. You'll become woven into the fabric of how they navigate this stage of their family's journey.

This level of trust is not built through systems. It emerges from presence—regular, sustained, and unrushed. You'll be accessible during morning arrival and afternoon departure, at significant evening gatherings, and in spontaneous one-on-one exchanges that rarely appear on schedules. You'll cultivate a community that families feel invested in protecting, where belonging is natural and referrals flow from the quality of the experience itself.

Alpha operates differently from conventional schools. Students complete core academics in two hours daily through AI-enabled applications, then dedicate remaining time to public speaking, critical thinking, and applied projects. No traditional lectures. No unnecessary assignments. Performance in the top 1% nationally. The families who enroll are investing in something they value deeply—but confidence requires ongoing reinforcement.

You'll sustain that dialogue with every family, through every question, for the duration of their enrollment. If the prospect of championing an unconventional approach to discerning skeptics feels daunting, this position is not suitable. If it represents the most compelling work you could pursue, continue reading.

What You Will Be Doing

Take ownership of the relationship with every family at your campus—the evolving, deepening connection that ensures parents feel recognized, not merely attended to
Identify potential concerns in advance and address them with the discretion and interpersonal skill that preserves confidence
Maintain visible presence in family spaces: morning arrival (7–9am), afternoon departure (2–4pm), evening parent gatherings, and select weekend programming
Develop a campus community with authentic depth—events, rituals, and touchpoints that foster a sense of belonging to something families consider worth safeguarding
Nurture parent champions organically, designing experiences of such consistent quality that referrals emerge naturally
Sustain the dialogue about Alpha's AI-driven model with families managing screen time questions, skeptical relatives, and periodic uncertainty—not only during enrollment but throughout their campus experience
Recognize when a family is not aligned and manage that reality with the same care you apply to all interactions—community protection is integral to service
Create the structures and routines this role demands in a setting where established protocols are still emerging

What You Won’t Be Doing

Overseeing enrollment processes—that falls under the Admissions Director; your responsibility begins after families enroll and intensifies over time
Operating from an office—your community presence defines the role
Maintaining standard 9–5 schedules—early mornings, late afternoons, and evening programming are routine components, not occasional requirements
Relying on administrative infrastructure, standardized procedures, or a complete team—you function with significant independence and create what is necessary
Managing family relationships as transactional accounts—this role values authentic engagement over procedural adherence

Director Of Partnerships And Community Outreach Key Responsibilities

Serve as the central trusted relationship in every family's Alpha experience. Maintain their confidence during challenging periods, cultivate a community where belonging feels meaningful, and establish conditions under which satisfied families naturally become outspoken advocates.

Basic Requirements

Currently living within commuting range of one of the following campuses OR prepared to relocate within 60 days (preference given to candidates with established local community ties): Denver, CO; Greenwich, CT; Boca Raton, FL; Tampa, FL; Boston, MA; Bethesda, MD; Charlotte, NC; Las Vegas, NV; Oklahoma City, OK; Tulsa, OK; Nashville, TN; Austin, TX; Dallas (Carrollton), TX; Fort Worth, TX; Houston (The Woodlands), TX; Park City, UT; Burlington, VT; Kirkland, WA; or Jackson Hole, WY
Authorized to work legally in the United States without sponsorship requirements
5+ years in relationship-focused roles serving high-net-worth or ultra-high-net-worth families—private school admissions, luxury hospitality, philanthropic development, or any context where discretion, warmth, and social acuity were equally critical to competence
Proven capacity to preserve relationships through complex situations with sophisticated families and maintain trust throughout
Authentic presence and communication approach that builds confidence rapidly with affluent, high-expectation families—genuine rather than rehearsed
Direct familiarity with independent, private, or alternative education—through professional work or parenting—that provides credible standing when families challenge the model
Authentic conviction in AI-enabled education and capacity to sustain that belief genuinely across years of parent engagement, not merely during initial enrollment
Flexibility for variable hours: early mornings (7–9am), late afternoons (2–4pm), evening events 2–3 times monthly, and occasional weekend activities

Nice-to-have Requirements

Exceptional candidates will possess at least one of these qualities:

Established connections within the local affluent community providing immediate, organic credibility on campus
Private school or independent school background with demonstrated success in family retention and community building—beyond enrollment metrics alone
Experience managing high-stakes family dynamics where your discretion and judgment were instrumental in preserving trust
Natural ability to design experiences—gatherings, moments, settings—where participants feel connected to something they value protecting
Prior startup or early-stage organizational experience where you built from the ground up and maintained composure amid rapid change

About Alpha

Join the team building school around student growth

Alpha reimagines the school day around faster learning, stronger mentorship, and more time for students to build real-world skills.

Roles with real student impact:
Alpha hires teachers (guides), operators, admissions experts, school & curriculum leaders, and remote team members.

Education work that pays like it matters:
Alpha offers six-figure roles for people ready to help build a radically better school experience.

A culture built for builders:
Alpha is fast-moving, ambitious, and student focused. It’s for people who want to improve the system, not maintain it.

Alpha is where you can do the most meaningful work of your career.

There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. Hit the Apply button if you found this interesting and want to learn more. We look forward to meeting you!

Working with us

Join our team at one of our senior living communities offering independent living, assisted living, or memory care, where we put Residents First while being team-focused and quality-centered. We put Residents First, and our team members matter! At Sagora, we invest in and empower our team members to move upward within the company and in their careers. Based on team member surveys, we are certified as a Great Place to Work! Apply now to join our dedicated team that cares, just like you!

Our Core Values
are Commitment, Empowerment, Communication, Excellence, and Teamwork!

Did you know that our name Sagora comes from the combination of two words – Sage, which means wisdom or wise person, and Agora, which means a gathering place. Our communities are a gathering place of wisdom!

Lasting relationships with our residents who have so much wisdom to share!

Position Details

Community name: The Brennity at Tradition
City, State: 10685 SW Stony Creek Way
Status: Full-Time
Shift/hours: Monday - Friday 8a-5p with rotating weekends as Manager on Duty and on-call 24hrs

Responsibilities

Oversee all operations and departments within the community, including resident care, business office, sales, culinary, housekeeping, activities, and maintenance
Oversee management of all team members, including recruiting, training, discipline, and coaching
Build relationships with residents and families
Report to the Regional Director VP with assigned reporting metrics

Skills/Requirements

Three (3) or more years of previous management experience in the senior housing industry (AL, MC, or LTC)
High School diploma or equivalent required, a college degree is preferred
Advanced computer and Microsoft Office experience with the ability to create reports and analyze operational issues
Excellent verbal and written communication skills and able to communicate effectively with residents, families, and other team members.
A desire to work with senior adults
Strong leadership skills and experience leading large teams with multiple departments
Experience overseeing the financial success of multiple departments
Ability to solve complex operational and people problems

jobs.title: Director of Community Engagement, Alpha - $200,000/year USD
jobs.description:
On-site (multiple locations—refer to Requirements)
W2 Employee
$200K annually

Families who select Alpha have trusted advisors embedded across every aspect of their lives. This is the relationship they haven't had—until now.

You will become the person they reach out to when uncertainty arises, when something doesn't sit right, or when they want to share a milestone they know you'll appreciate. You'll understand each family deeply enough to recognize their needs before they articulate them—and you'll possess the judgment and composure to address what they bring you with care, warmth, and seamless professionalism. Over time, you won't simply be associated with their child's education. You'll be woven into the way they experience this formative period in their family's journey.

That level of trust isn't engineered through procedures. It's cultivated through presence—daily, reliable, and unhurried. You'll be on-site during morning arrival and afternoon departure, at the evening gatherings that hold meaning, and in the spontaneous one-on-one exchanges that never appear on a schedule. You'll shape a community that families feel invested in protecting, where a sense of belonging comes naturally and word-of-mouth referrals emerge because the experience justifies them.

Alpha operates outside the traditional school framework. Students complete their core academics in two hours daily via AI-driven applications, then dedicate the remainder of their time to public speaking, critical reasoning, and applied projects. No lectures. No redundant assignments. Top 1% outcomes nationally. The families who commit to it are investing in something they value deeply—but conviction requires ongoing stewardship.

You'll sustain that dialogue with every family, through every moment of uncertainty, for the entire duration of their child's enrollment. If advocating for an unconventional approach to discerning skeptics feels taxing, this position won't suit you. If it sounds like the most compelling conversation you could be leading, continue reading.

What You Will Be Doing

Take ownership of the relationship with every family at your campus—the sustained, evolving relationship that ensures parents feel understood, not simply attended to
Foresee concerns before they surface as issues, and address them with the tact and interpersonal skill that preserves confidence
Maintain visible presence in the spaces families occupy: morning arrivals (7–9am), afternoon departures (2–4pm), evening parent gatherings, and occasional weekend programming
Develop a campus culture with authentic depth—rituals, events, and touchpoints that give families the sense they're part of something valuable and worth safeguarding
Foster parent advocacy naturally, engineering experiences of such consistent quality that referrals emerge as an organic result
Sustain the ongoing dialogue about Alpha's AI-enabled approach with families managing screen time concerns, skeptical relatives, and periods of uncertainty—not only during enrollment, but continuously throughout their campus tenure
Recognize when a family is not aligned with the community and manage that transition with the same thoughtfulness you apply everywhere else—protecting the community is integral to serving it
Establish the systems and routines this position demands in a setting where the framework is still evolving

What You Won’t Be Doing

Overseeing enrollment—that belongs to the Admissions Director; your work commences when families become part of the community and expands from there
Remaining desk-bound—your physical presence within the community defines the role
Adhering to standard 9–5 schedules—early mornings, late afternoons, and evening commitments are inherent to the position, not occasional demands
Relying on administrative infrastructure, established protocols, or a built-out team—you function with significant autonomy and construct what's necessary
Approaching family relationships as transactional accounts—this role rewards authentic connection, not procedural adherence

Director Of Community Engagement Key Responsibilities

Serve as the central trusted relationship within every family's Alpha experience. Maintain their confidence through challenging moments, cultivate a community where belonging feels meaningful, and establish the environment in which satisfied families naturally become active advocates.

Basic Requirements

Currently live within commuting range of one of these locations OR prepared to relocate within 60 days (preference given to candidates with existing local community ties): Denver, CO; Greenwich, CT; Boca Raton, FL; Tampa, FL; Boston, MA; Bethesda, MD; Charlotte, NC; Las Vegas, NV; Oklahoma City, OK; Tulsa, OK; Nashville, TN; Austin, TX; Dallas (Carrollton), TX; Fort Worth, TX; Houston (The Woodlands), TX; Park City, UT; Burlington, VT; Kirkland, WA; or Jackson Hole, WY
Authorized to work in the United States without requiring sponsorship
5+ years in a relationship-centered role serving high-net-worth or ultra-high-net-worth families—contexts such as private school admissions, luxury hospitality, philanthropy, or environments where discretion, warmth, and interpersonal intelligence were as critical as competence
Proven capacity to maintain relationships through challenging moments with sophisticated families and exit with trust strengthened
Authentic presence and communication approach that establishes credibility quickly with affluent, high-expectation families—polished in a way that feels genuine, not rehearsed
Personal experience with independent, private, or alternative education—either professionally or as a parent—that provides you credible perspective when families challenge the model
Authentic conviction in AI-enabled education and the capacity to carry that belief genuinely through years of parent engagement, not solely during the enrollment phase
Availability for nontraditional hours: early mornings (7–9am), late afternoons (2–4pm), evening functions 2–3 times monthly, and occasional weekend activities

Nice-to-have Requirements

Exceptional candidates will possess at least one of the following:

Established connections within the local affluent community that would provide you immediate, organic credibility on campus
Private or independent school background with demonstrated success in family retention and community cohesion—not merely enrollment metrics
Experience managing high-stakes family dynamics where your judgment and discretion were the foundation of sustained trust
A natural talent for designing experiences—gatherings, moments, settings—where people sense they belong to something meaningful and protective
Prior startup or early-stage involvement where you built infrastructure from scratch and maintained composure amid rapid change

About Alpha

Join the team building school around student growth

Alpha reimagines the school day around faster learning, stronger mentorship, and more time for students to build real-world skills.

Roles with real student impact:
Alpha hires teachers (guides), operators, admissions experts, school & curriculum leaders, and remote team members.

Education work that pays like it matters:
Alpha offers six-figure roles for people ready to help build a radically better school experience.

A culture built for builders:
Alpha is fast-moving, ambitious, and student focused. It’s for people who want to improve the system, not maintain it.

Alpha is where you can do the most meaningful work of your career.

There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. Hit the Apply button if you found this interesting and want to learn more. We look forward to meeting you!

Working with us

Our powerhouse team of attorneys, admitted in over 40 courts nationwide, combines deep expertise with innovative litigation strategies to tackle some of the largest, most complex cases in the country.

We are relentless in holding corporations and their executives accountable for consumer protection violations, securing significant settlements and landmark legal precedents that protect individuals and shape industry behavior.

As the Director of Operations for Levi & Korsinsky LLP, you will play a pivotal role in propelling the firm’s growth trajectory in consumer related class and mass actions. Tasked with transforming strategic vision into operational reality, you will work closely alongside, and in many ways, act as the extension of, the Founder to take ownership of key initiatives, solve organizational challenges, streamline operations, and help drive execution across the business. Your role is crucial in aligning the firm's innovative practices with its overarching mission of empowering consumers and optimizing the firm’s high-stakes business approach.

This role is not stepping into a mature machine. The firm’s securities practice has
been around for 20+ years, you will be working on growing the consumer practice
and its associated verticals that sits alongside the securities practice. You will be part
of a team helping build stronger systems while driving immediate execution in a fast-moving, evolving environment.

What you’ll do:

Operational Execution:
Drive execution of strategies across the firm, ensuring alignment between strategy and day-to-day execution. Identify and address inefficiencies, strengthen workflows, and help ensure seamless operations.
HR and Talent Strategy:
Build and optimize the firm’s recruiting engine and people systems, including passive talent sourcing, screening rigor, onboarding, and performance accountability, helping strengthen the team infrastructure needed to support growth.
Marketing Oversight:
Provide strategic oversight across marketing efforts, ensuring campaigns are aligned, properly targeted, and executed effectively, while helping identify breakdowns in process, quality, and performance.
Systems, Technology, and AI:
Evaluate and implement tools, automation, and systems that improve efficiency, strengthen workflows, and support smarter, faster execution across the business.
Cross-Functional Leadership:
Coordinate execution across legal, operations, marketing, offshore teams, and external partners, creating clear alignment, stronger accountability, and more effective execution across a complex, globally distributed organization.
Problem Solving & Execution:
Take ownership of complex, ambiguous challenges, identify practical solutions, and drive decisions forward with sound judgment and decisive action.
Build and Scale in Motion:
Help strengthen systems, solve breakdowns, and build infrastructure while the business continues to grow, driving execution in a fast-moving environment where priorities shift quickly, complexity is constant, and maintaining momentum is critical.

What we’re looking for:

Operational Leadership:
Extensive experience leading and scaling operations in a high-growth, fast-moving, entrepreneurial environment; startup or growth-stage operating experience strongly preferred.
Operator Mindset:
Demonstrated ability to step into complex, evolving environments, bring structure to ambiguity, and drive execution with a high degree of ownership and sound judgment.
Strategic Insight:
Proven ability to identify patterns across functions, anticipate challenges, and develop scalable solutions that strengthen systems and improve organizational performance.
Financial and Operational Acumen:
Comfort using KPIs, data, and ROI thinking to evaluate tradeoffs, improve performance, and support sound operating decisions.
Change Leadership and Influence:
Ability to earn trust, navigate resistance, and drive change across strong personalities and cross-functional teams through credibility, judgment, and execution.
Recruitment Expertise:
Proven record in building and managing talent acquisition and retention systems for high-performing teams.
Global and Vendor Coordination:
Experience leading through offshore teams, external partners, or distributed operating environments, with strong instincts for coordination, accountability, and quality control.
AI and Automation Fluency:
Strong familiarity with AI, automation, and emerging tools, with the ability to leverage technology to improve efficiency and execution.
Problem-Solving Ability:
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to assess complex challenges, exercise sound judgment, and drive practical solutions under pressure.

Why you should work here:

Leadership Collaboration:
Direct engagement with the firm’s leadership offers opportunities for mentorship and impactful decision-making.
Innovative Environment:
Work within a pioneering firm that values innovation, technology, and cutting-edge legal strategies.
Global Influence:
Participate in high-visibility projects that make a tangible impact on clients and global legal practices.
Empowerment and Autonomy:
Enjoy a significant degree of autonomy in decision-making and strategy implementation, while contributing to the firm’s legacy.

This is a unique opportunity to join a forward-thinking firm where your contributions will drive meaningful change and promote the firm’s mission of legal excellence. If you are a strategic, results-driven leader ready to make an impact, we invite you to apply and become part of our innovative journey.

new_jobs.title: Director of Partnerships
new_jobs.description:
About Pagaya

Shape the Future of Finance

Pagaya is building a leading artificial intelligence network to help our partners grow their businesses and better serve their customers.

Pagaya is a global technology company making life-changing financial products and services available to more people nationwide, as it reshapes the financial services ecosystem. By using machine learning, a vast data network and a sophisticated AI-driven approach, Pagaya provides comprehensive consumer credit and residential real estate solutions for its partners, their customers, and investors. Its proprietary API and capital solutions integrate into its network of partners to deliver seamless user experiences and greater access to the mainstream economy. Pagaya has offices in New York and Tel Aviv. For more information, visit pagaya.com.

Let's create better outcomes together!

About The Role

The Director of Partnerships at Pagaya will lead and expand strategic relationships across personal loan originators, fintech platforms, and bank partners at an executive level. This role is responsible for shaping partner strategy, driving revenue and profitability, and executing key initiatives to accelerate growth across Pagaya’s personal loan network. The position will play a critical role in scaling partnerships, identifying new business opportunities, and delivering measurable impact across volume, revenue, margin, and strategic outcomes.

Responsibilities

Industry & Partner Expertise: Serve as a subject matter expert within Pagaya on the personal loans ecosystem, including fintech lenders, digital platforms, and bank partnerships. Maintain a deep understanding of market dynamics, competitive landscape, and partner business models.
Business Delivery: Own and deliver on core partner KPIs, including origination volume, revenue growth, profitability, partner performance, and execution of strategic initiatives.
Business, Product & Solution Development: Identify and drive new opportunities within existing and prospective partners. Lead commercial discussions, negotiate agreements, and collaborate cross-functionally (including Product, Data, and Risk teams) to design and deliver tailored solutions that meet partner needs and unlock growth.
Strategy Development: Develop and execute comprehensive partner strategies and account plans, incorporating partner insights, long-term vision, growth opportunities, risks, KPI targets, and relationship development roadmaps.
Key Relationship Development: Build and maintain strong executive-level relationships across fintechs, banks, and ecosystem partners. Establish trusted partnerships that enable long-term success and collaboration.
Partner Communication: Lead all partner communications, including business reviews, strategic discussions, escalations, and negotiations. Ensure clear, consistent messaging and alignment across all engagements.
Internal Stakeholder Management: Partner cross-functionally across internal teams- including Legal, Product, Capital Markets, Operations, Risk, and Analytics - to ensure seamless execution, alignment, and issue resolution.
Leadership Updates & Preparation - Provide regular updates to senior leadership on partner performance, risks, and opportunities. Prepare executives for key partner interactions and strategic discussions.

Requirements

10+ years of experience managing strategic B2B partnerships, client relationships, and/or business development, preferably within fintech, consumer lending, or financial services.
Strong experience in personal loans, unsecured consumer lending, fintech platforms, and/or bank partnerships.
Proven ability to develop and execute partner strategies that drive measurable business growth.
Demonstrated success in managing and growing executive-level relationships.
Experience identifying, negotiating, and closing new business opportunities within existing or new accounts.
Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills, with the ability to evaluate complex business opportunities and partner performance.
Experience working cross-functionally in fast-paced, technology-driven environments.
Ability to influence and align multiple stakeholders across internal and external teams.
Excellent communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts and drive alignment.
Strong financial acumen, including experience with P&L management (or capital markets), unit economics, and performance metrics.
Experience managing global or cross-cultural teams is a plus.

The pay ranges for New York-based hires are commensurate with candidate experience.

Pay ranges for candidates working in locations other than New York may differ based on the cost of labor in that location.

Compensation Range for New York Based Hires

$200,000—$240,000 USD

Our Team

Pagaya was founded in 2016 by seasoned research, finance, and technology entrepreneurs with our head quarters located in NYC and Tel Aviv.

We move fast and smart, identifying new opportunities and building end-to-end solutions from AI models and unique data sources. Every Pagaya team member is solving new and exciting challenges every day in a culture based on partnership, collaboration, and community.

Join a team of builders who are working every day to enable better outcomes for our partners and their customers.

Our Values

Continuously Learn- We challenge ourselves for the sake of getting better as individuals, as teams, and as an organization to deliver for our partners.
Debate and Commit- We respectfully and openly debate to strengthen our ideas and build shared conviction - once we decide, we go all in, together.
Dream Big and Act- We boldly tackle complex problems, pressure-test solutions in real-time, and adapt with speed and energy.
Advance Inclusion- We create a world where everyone can win, designing systems that better represent people and generate sustainable value for our employees, partners and investors.
Be Accountable Together- We proudly own our actions and our results, taking initiative to ensure our work gets over the finish line as a team.

More than just a job

We believe health, happiness, and productivity go hand-in-hand. That's why we're continually looking to enhance the ways we support you with benefits programs and perks that allow every Pagayan to do the best work of their life.

new_jobs.title: Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner
new_jobs.description:
What We Can Achieve Together

The Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner functions as a leader and conservation strategist for a large, complex business unit in a global organization that plays an integral leadership role in TNC’s Midwest Division. The Midwest Division encompasses Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio and Wisconsin—a region of Great Lakes, great rivers and immense global importance for conserving biodiversity, tackling climate change, and protecting lands and waters as outlined in TNC’s 2030 goals. In addition to representing Michigan and the Midwest, this position must also be versed in national and global TNC priorities.

This position will be a key leader in a global fundraising campaign and is responsible for producing measurable conservation results, while leading a large, multidisciplinary team of professionals across the state. They serve as the primary spokesperson for TNC in Michigan to internal and external audiences (including public and private donors, media, the business community, the Michigan Board of Trustees, community leaders and other partners, government agencies and officials and staff). They cultivate those audiences to support and promote TNC’s mission and vision.

The Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner sets objectives and manages the performance of a large and collaborative team with a focus on strategy development, fundraising and managing the Board of Trustees. They work together across teams as they cultivate and solicit high-level donors (private and public) to meet fundraising goals. Additionally, this role supports conservation programs and initiatives, organization visibility, policy and government relations, the management of staff and the financial health of the business unit.

The Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner also collaborates with peer Midwest Executive Directors and the Midwest Division Director (known as the Midwest Board of Partners) in helping shape the strategy, development and execution of cross-boundary efforts within the Midwest and globally, including the contribution of financial and human resources. Along with other members of the Midwest Board of Partners, the Michigan Executive Director supports the Midwest 2030 conservation goals, and sponsors one or more conservation strategy and functional area teams at the regional level. They help align their team’s work to secure and deploy sufficient resources and capacity to achieve the Midwest’s maximum contribution to TNC’s 2030 conservation goals.

The Michigan Executive Director and Midwest Partner creates a healthy work environment for all TNC staff in the state and supports coordinated staff development across the region. This position reports to the Midwest Division Director and works in partnership with the Midwest Board of Partners and the Michigan Board of Trustees.

Location:
Within Michigan, preference for the greater Lansing, Ann Arbor, Detroit, or Grand Rapids areas.

We’re Looking For You

Are you looking for work that you can believe in? At TNC, we strive to embody a philosophy of “Work That You Can Believe In” where you can feel like you are making a difference every day. We’re looking for someone with strong experience leading a diverse, multidisciplinary team.

The ideal candidate should have exceptional leadership skills, with a strong background in high-level fundraising. They must be a compelling storyteller and talented communicator who can successfully use these talents to fundraise, build consensus and speak about TNC’s mission, vision and work—from state, regional and global perspective. This role must be able to effectively engage a wide range of partners, while constantly building and engaging the volunteer Board of Trustees.

This position requires a person with deep self-confidence tempered by genuine humility. They must have an ambitious yet collaborative nature. They must be very high-energy, intelligent, abstract thinkers willing to take bold, calculated risks while balancing the need to make transparent decisions in a matrixed and sometimes ambiguous environment. They must have very high emotional intelligence and be compassionate but also able to make tough decisions while being equitable, fair and inclusive.

This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to the ongoing mission of conservation by directing and shaping the work of Michigan and the Midwest.

What You’ll Bring

Bachelor’s degree and minimum of 7 years of experience as a leader in the conservation arena, non-profit sector, advocacy, or related for-profit area.
Extensive management experience, including the ability to motivate, lead, set objectives and manage the performance of a large multidisciplinary team.
Proven experience in cultivating and soliciting high-level donors to reach fundraising goals, which is a priority for this position.
Written and verbal fluency in English.
Effective presentation and communication skills for diverse audiences, including high-level donors, the media, the business community, board members, outside partners, staff or equivalent that lead to desired outcomes.
Experience with strategic fiscal management, including long-term sustainability.

Desired Qualifications

Seven to 10 years of experience as a proven leader in the conservation arena, nonprofit sector, advocacy, or related for-profit area, including demonstrated experience producing results and meeting organizational, divisional or business unit goals.
Experience working in the U.S. and globally preferred. Demonstrated commitment to developing and practicing global literacy.
Outstanding leadership skills, including thought-leadership, visionary qualities and the ability to inspire others in a decentralized, geographically dispersed and highly matrixed organization.
Knowledge of conservation and conservation practices.
High energy, forward-thinking and creative individual with high ethical standards.
Proven strategic thinking skills, creative problem-solving, adept decision-making, adaptability and ability to collaborate and influence.
Demonstrated experience in public speaking on behalf of a global organization with the capability to present on a wide range of science-based and conservation topics desired.
A strong network of local, regional and national contacts that include leaders from philanthropic, business and communities relevant to our conservation work.

This position requires a valid driver's license and compliance with TNC’s Auto Safety Program
. Employees may not drive Conservancy-owned/leased vehicles, rental cars, or personal vehicles on behalf of the Conservancy if considered "high risk drivers." To learn more about the Auto Safety Program, visit the Auto Safety information page. Employment in this position will be contingent upon completion of a Vehicle Use Agreement, which may include a review of the prospective employee's motor vehicle record.

Salary Information

The starting pay range for a candidate selected for this position is generally within the range of $185,000 - $218,000. This range only applies to candidates whose country of employment is the USA. Where a successful candidate’s actual pay will fall within this range will be based on a variety of factors, including, for example, the candidate's location, qualifications, specific skills, and experience. Please note countries outside the USA would have a different pay range in the local currency based on the local labor market, and not tied to USA pay or ranges. Your geographic location will be confirmed during the recruitment.

Who We Are

The Nature Conservancy’s mission is to protect the lands and waters upon which all life depends. As a science-based organization, we create innovative, on-the-ground solutions to our world’s toughest challenges so that we can create a world in which people and nature thrive. We’re rooted in our mission and guided by our values, which include respect for all people, communities, and cultures. Whether it’s career development, flexible schedules, or a rewarding mission, there’s many reasons to love life inside TNC. Want a better insight to TNC? Check out our TNC Talent playlist on YouTube to hear stories from staff or visit Glassdoor.

One goal is to cultivate an inclusive work environment so that all our colleagues around the globe feel a sense of belonging and that their unique contributions to our mission are valued. In addition to the requirements in our job postings, we recognize that people come with talent and experiences outside of a job and consider each applicant’s unique experience. Please apply – we’d love to hear from you. To quote a popular saying at TNC, “you’ll join for the mission, and stay for the people.”

What We Bring

Since 1951, TNC has been doing work you can believe in. Through grassroots action, we have grown from a small non-profit into one of the most effective and wide-reaching environmental organizations in the world. Thanks to more than 1 million members, over 400 scientists, and the dedicated efforts of our diverse staff and partners, we impact conservation around the world!

TNC offers a competitive, comprehensive benefits package including health care benefits, flexible spending accounts, a 401(k) plan with an 8% employer match, parental leave, accrued paid time off, life insurance, disability coverage, employee assistance program, other life and work well-being benefits. Learn more about our Benefits and Perks here.

We’re proud to offer a flexible work environment that supports of the health and well-being of the people we employ.

Our recruiting process includes a rolling interview process to ensure we engage applicants in a timely manner. This means we may review applications in the order in which they are received. Once a strong candidate pool is identified, the role will be unposted. The timeline may vary depending on the expressed interest in the role, so we highly encourage candidates to apply as soon as possible.

The Nature Conservancy is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
Our commitment to equal employment opportunity includes the recognition that our conservation mission is best advanced by the leadership and contributions of people of all backgrounds, beliefs, and culture. Recruiting and mentoring staff to create an inclusive organization is a priority, and we encourage applicants from all cultures, races, colors, religions, sexes, national or regional origins, ages, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity, military, protected veteran status or other status protected by law.

The successful applicant must meet the requirements of The Nature Conservancy's background screening process.

Do you have military experience? Visit our U.S. Military Skills Translator to match your military experience with our current job openings!

TNC is committed to offering accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application process. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please send a note to applyhelp@tnc.org with Request for Accommodation in the subject line.

Financial & Estate Literacy (It’s Your Money & Estate)

Website: itsyourmoneyandestate.org

Full-Time

Benefits:
Comprehensive benefits package

About Financial & Estate Literacy

Financial & Estate Literacy (It’s Your Money & Estate) is a mission-driven nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering individuals and families with the knowledge and resources needed to make informed financial and estate planning decisions.
Through educational programs, community workshops, and strategic partnerships, the organization works to increase financial confidence, promote responsible planning, philanthropic giving and help prevent financial exploitation through awareness and education.

Position Overview

The Executive Director serves as the chief executive leader of Financial & Estate Literacy and is responsible for advancing the organization’s mission through strategic leadership, effective governance partnership, program excellence, community engagement, and operational stewardship.
Working closely with the Board of Directors, the Executive Director will translate the organization’s mission and strategic priorities into measurable outcomes while ensuring strong governance practices, financial sustainability, operational effectiveness, and continued program growth.
The ideal candidate is a collaborative, mission-focused leader who can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution and build meaningful relationships with donors, volunteers, educators, professional advisors, community organizations, and other stakeholders.
This is an exciting opportunity for a nonprofit leader who is passionate about education, financial empowerment, and creating lasting community impact.

Key Responsibilities

Executive Leadership & Strategic Direction

• Provide visionary leadership and strategic direction to advance the organization’s mission and long-term goals.
• Partner with the Board of Directors to develop and implement strategic plans, priorities, and initiatives.
• Translate organizational objectives into actionable programs, measurable outcomes, and sustainable growth strategies.
• Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, innovation, and continuous improvement.
Board Governance & Partnership

• Serve as the primary liaison between the Board of Directors and organizational operations.
• Support the Board in fulfilling its governance, fiduciary, and strategic responsibilities by providing timely, accurate, and meaningful information.

• Support Board recruitment, development, engagement, and ongoing education.

Program Leadership & Community Impact

and It’s Your Estate
programs.

• Expand educational outreach through workshops, partnerships, and innovative programming.
• Support facilitators, volunteers, and community partners in delivering high-quality educational experiences.
Operations & Organizational Management

• Oversee daily operations and ensure effective systems, processes, and infrastructure.
• Develop and maintain policies, procedures, and organizational practices that support accountability and efficiency.
• Coordinate staff, contractors, volunteers, vendors, and strategic partners.
• Ensure compliance with nonprofit standards and operational requirements.
Financial Stewardship & Sustainability

• Provide leadership for budgeting, financial planning, and responsible resource allocation.
• Work with the Board Treasurer and financial advisors to monitor financial performance and sustainability.
• Ensure sound financial practices, transparency, and responsible stewardship of organizational resources.
• Identify opportunities to strengthen revenue streams and long-term financial stability.
Fundraising & Resource Development

• Develop and execute fundraising strategies in partnership with the Board and fundraising committees.
• Cultivate relationships with donors, sponsors, foundations, corporations, and community supporters.
• Lead donor engagement, sponsorship development, grant opportunities, and stewardship efforts.
• Communicate organizational impact and funding priorities effectively.
Community Engagement & External Relations

• Build and maintain partnerships with professional advisors, nonprofits, educational institutions, corporations, and community organizations.
• Represent Financial & Estate Literacy at events, educational forums, and community meetings.

Qualifications

• Proven ability to develop strategy, manage operations, and lead organizational initiatives.
• Strong financial management and resource development experience.
• Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
• Ability to inspire and engage donors, volunteers, partners, and community stakeholders.
• Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively.
• Bachelor’s degree required.
Preferred Qualifications

• CFRE designation
• Experience leading educational programs, workshops, or community outreach initiatives.
• Experience with fundraising, grants, sponsorships, or nonprofit development.
• Knowledge of financial literacy, estate planning, elder empowerment, aging populations, or charitable giving education.
• Experience working with professional advisors, financial professionals, attorneys, or community organizations.
• Entrepreneurial m indset with the ability to identify opportunities for growth and innovation.

Work Environment & Benefits

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit a resume and letter of interest describing their leadership experience, nonprofit background, and connection to the mission of Financial & Estate Literacy to Patricia Collins
at patricia@itsyourmoneyandestate.org

new_jobs.title: Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
DIRECTOR OF DEVELOPMENT

The Lussier Community Education Center (LCEC) is a vibrant community center with a spirited history of strong programs, broad partnerships, and grassroots community organizing. Located on the grounds of Ezekiel Gillespie Middle School and Vel Phillips Memorial High School, the LCEC has a mission of building community, creating opportunities, and enriching education .  

The Director of Development
will lead the overall fund development efforts for the LCEC, building fund development capacity through a diverse array of strategies in support of a nearly two million dollar operating budget. The Director of Development will work closely with the Executive Director to design and implement the Center’s fund development strategies as well as guide the organization’s marketing and communication plan. The ideal candidate will have strong writing and communication skills, understand the power of intentional relationships, and approach the work through a community-centric fundraising lens. As a member of the LCEC Leadership Team, the Director of Development plays an important role in guiding the growth of the Center toward fulfillment of its mission in alignment with the Lussier Framework .

Strong candidates will be creative, organized, flexible, and have experiential knowledge of fund development in nonprofits. They will be able to manage a diverse set of hands-on responsibilities and projects, while supporting and connecting with an effective, independent staff team in a grassroots, collaborative, community setting. The Director of Development reports to the Executive Director.  

Hours:
Full-time, on-site at West-side Madison location, generally 9 am - 5 pm M-F with some later evening and weekend hours required, as well as occasional off-site meetings and events as needed.

Compensation:
$74,000/year

Benefits:
100% employer-paid premium for health insurance plus a health reimbursement arrangement; competitive paid time-off package that includes pay for vacation, personal days, sick leave, and holidays; dental insurance; short- and long-term disability insurance; life insurance; 403(b); and flexible spending account.  

RESPONSIBILITIES  

Fund Development Management

Develop, update, and evaluate the LCEC Fund Development Plan with the Executive Director and lead its execution.
Monitor and communicate progress towards the annual fundraising goal to the Executive Director and Board of Directors.
Develop and maintain the systems needed to support the execution of the LCEC Fund Development Plan including the master fund development calendar, application and reporting deadlines, etc.

Individual and Business Donor Development

Manage and grow individual donor support through intentional and continued relationship building and relational meetings.
Maintain and expand the base of donors, potential donors, local businesses, service and school groups, and faith based organizations supporting the LCEC and our programs.
Work with the Executive Director to maintain and grow the base of major/recurring donors.
Maintain and evaluate the organization’s donor database and communications platforms.
Process and acknowledge all donations: log checks, prepare deposits, generate tax receipts, and ensure thank yous are created in collaboration with the Executive Director, staff, Board, volunteers, and program participants.
Organize donor events (Harvest, Friends of Lussier breakfasts, etc.) to raise resources, build relationships, and create community.
Explore opportunities for planned giving, stock and securities, cryptocurrency, property/estate, in-kind, and all other revenue generation resources.  

Grant, Foundation, and Sponsorship Development and Management

Ensure completion of proposals and regular program reporting to major institutional funders ( City of Madison, Dane County, United Way, CACFP, Second Harvest Kids Cafe, etc. )  
Identify and pursue funding opportunities through grants, foundations, local, state, and federal government, and other revenue generating resources.
Write timely, accurate, and effective grant proposals and ensure timely reporting.
Seek out and lead the development of collaborative grant proposals with partner organizations.  
Work with staff to identify matches between LCEC programs and grant opportunities.
Coordinate with directors and managers to ensure the timely completion of grant requirements and reports.
