new_jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
new_jobs.description:
CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER

SPY is looking for a leader who can guide an established youth homelessness organization through its next stage of scale, sustainability, housing growth, public advocacy, and strategic planning. The next leader must have an unwavering commitment to championing SPY's mission and the young people it serves; the ability to adaptively support and develop SPY staff; effectively lead a robust fundraising, external relations, and finance management strategy; and the experience to oversee a complex $15M organization operating 24 hour programs.

ABOUT SAFE PLACE FOR YOUTH

Safe Place for Youth is a leading youth homelessness organization serving young people ages 12 to 25 who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness in Los Angeles. SPY’s mission is to prevent and end youth homelessness through supportive services, sustainable housing opportunities, community collaboration, and advocacy, with a vision of a socially just and equitable world where all young people are empowered to thrive in self-directed lives and have a safe place to call home.

SPY provides a continuum of youth-centered services that includes street outreach, a drop-in center, case management, education and employment, health and wellness, healing arts, community garden, pregnant and parenting support, shelter, social enterprise, family reunification, housing, and advocacy. In 2025, SPY served more than 1,200 youth, helped 315 young people move into housing, and connected hundreds more to mental health counseling, case management, workforce training, creative arts, street outreach, and other critical supports.

SPY has entered a new stage of organizational complexity and opportunity. The organization now operates 10 sites across the County, including multiple housing programs across shelter, transitional housing, and permanent housing, providing 224 housing resources on any given night. SPY also provides robust supportive services through a field-based model and out of our drop-in center in Venice, including basic needs resources, case management, mental health, substance use counseling, education and employment, pregnant and parenting, healing arts and more.        

The next leader will be called upon to sustain operational excellence across housing and services programs, actively lead a robust fundraising strategy, ensure strong financial oversight, continue SPY’s leadership in advocacy, maintain strong community collaboration, support strong recruitment, retention and development of staff, while keeping the dignity and wellbeing of youth at the center of all of SPY’s work.      

POSITION OVERVIEW

SPY seeks a Chief Executive Officer
to lead the organization into its next phase of impact, sustainability, and public leadership.

The next leader will inherit a respected, mission-driven organization with a strong community identity, a seasoned senior team, an engaged Board, a growing housing portfolio, a newly redeveloped drop-in center, a planned capital campaign, and an urgent opportunity to shape the future of youth homelessness policy and practice in Los Angeles. The role requires a leader who can hold multiple responsibilities at once: stewarding a complex nonprofit enterprise, strengthening internal systems, serving as an external advocate and fundraiser, leading through a new strategic plan, and ensuring that SPY remains deeply connected to the young people and communities it serves.

The successful candidate will be a values-driven, emotionally intelligent, operationally capable, and externally credible leader who can balance ambition with sustainability. They will bring the strategic judgment to decide where SPY should grow, where it should consolidate, where it should advocate, and where it should say no. They will be equally comfortable with youth, staff, donors, elected officials, public agency leaders, community partners, and the Board.
WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Strategic Leadership:
Develop and execute strategic plans in alignment with the organization’s mission, vision, and values. Lead the organization in identifying trends, funding environment, challenges, and future growth opportunities in the housing environment.
Advocacy and Policy Engagement:
Serve as an external champion, advocate, and coalition builder for SPY. Build and maintain strong relationships with elected officials, public agencies, funders, community stakeholders, peer organizations, housing partners, researchers, advocates, neighborhood leaders, and media. Advocate for youth-specific homelessness funding, policy, housing resources, and service models.
Program and Housing Leadership:
Oversee the continued development of SPY’s continuum of care, including outreach, drop-in services, case management, education and employment, health and wellness, healing arts, pregnant and parenting supports, advocacy, shelter, transitional housing, permanent housing, and housing navigation.
Fundraising and Resource Development:

Lead fundraising efforts to secure financial support from diverse sources, including grants, donations, and corporate giving, and other forms of sponsorships.

Cultivate, solicit, and steward relationships with donors, foundations, and corporate partners to expand and diversify the organization's funding base and ensure financial sustainability.

Design expansion and business planning for program expansion. Build partnerships in new areas, establishing relationship with funders and political and community leaders for expansion sites.

Financial Management and Enterprise Sustainability:
Oversee SPY’s financial health in partnership with the CFO, Board Treasurer, Finance Committee, and senior leadership. Ensure strong budgeting, forecasting, cash flow management, reserves planning, audit readiness, internal controls, contract compliance, and financial reporting.
Staff Leadership and Culture:
Provide effective leadership and management of staff, volunteers, and consultants that fosters a collaborative and inclusive work environment while still maintaining accountability.
Board Relations and Governance:
Work closely with the Board of Directors to ensure effective board operations, governance, accountability, and transparency. Provide regular updates, reports, and recommendations to the Board to facilitate informed decision-making and strategic planning.
Other duties as assigned to support the mission of the organization.

REQUIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE YOU’LL NEED:

At least 5 years of executive leadership experience
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience required; advanced degree or relevant professional training preferred
Significant senior leadership experience in a nonprofit, public agency, housing, social services, youth-serving, advocacy, or mission-driven organization.
Demonstrated commitment to racial equity, social justice, LGBTQ+ inclusion, trauma-informed practice, and youth voice.
Ability to embody SPY’s values of care, community, inclusivity, innovation, integrity, and sustainability.
Demonstrated ability to lead teams, manage complexity, and build organizational infrastructure.
Experience with fundraising, external relations, public funding, and     institutional partnerships.
Strong financial literacy and experience managing or overseeing budgets, audits, contracts, and organizational risk.
Demonstrated adaptive and situational leadership, and experience mentoring and developing teams. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Past success working with a Board of Directors with the ability to cultivate board member relationships

WORK ENVIRONMENT AND PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS:

Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

Hybrid work environment, such as shared office space and/or remote work, with meaningful engagement at SPY sites.

Physical effort/lifting, up to 15-20 pounds at times.

SPECIAL CONDITIONS:

Mandated Reporting Requirements:
The person holding this position is considered a ‘mandated reporter’ under the California Child Abuse and Neglect Reporting Act and is required to comply with the requirements as a condition of employment.

BENEFITS & PERKS:

Safe Place for Youth offers a competitive benefit package including comprehensive health coverage, 403b with agency contribution, and vacation/sick time. We also offer unique perks including weekly meditation/mindfulness and professional development opportunities that are tailored to your individual career growth. 

COMPENSATION:

Salary is $210,000.00 to $230,000.00, commiserate with experience.

EEO STATEMENT:

All aspects of employment including the decision to hire, promote, discipline, or discharge, will be based on merit, competence, performance, and business needs. Safe Place for Youth does not discriminate on the basis of any protected status under federal, state, or local law. 

Safe Place for Youth is an equal opportunity employer committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce. In addition, the organization will consider employment-qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of the Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring.

Please include references in your application.

Location: Southeast United States (Remote/hybrid options available}

About Us

US Catholic Bishop's Southeast Regional Office for Hispanic Ministry, also known as SEPI, coordinates and supports Hispanic Ministry in the 30 Catholic dioceses of the Southeast United States (Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee). Through its three pillars of Evangelization, Formation and Communion, SEPI strives to facilitate the full participation of Hispanics/Latinos in the mission of the Catholic Church and society as authentic missionary disciples. SEPI was born in 1979 as a response to the need for Hispanic education as recommended by the II National Hispanic Pastoral Meeting. SEPl's mission and values are to form and accompany Catholic leaders and communities in the Southeast by fostering communion, formation, and evangelization, grounded in the lived faith of Hispanic/Latino communities and in service to the Church's mission today.

Position Overview

SEPI is seeking a Director of Development to lead fundraising initiatives, cultivate donor relationships, and oversee all aspects of securing gift resources for the organization. This position is crucial for advancing SEP l's mission by securing philanthropic support and building sustainable relationships with individuals, foundations, and corporations. This person works directly with the SEPI Executive Director and works closely with key leadership and staff to design and implement strategies that drive philanthropic growth and sustainability.

This opportunity is ideal for someone motivated by the challenge to start and grow an effective, comprehensive development program. Prior nonprofit fundraising experience is preferred, but it is not required for candidates who demonstrate strong relationship-building instincts, a passion for purpose driven work and the ability to clearly communicate the value of philanthropy.

Key Functions

Develop and execute a comprehensive annual development plan with achievable goals and objectives. This plan should include activities in each of the key areas of a comprehensive development effort: major gifts, annual giving, special events and estate gifts. This plan will need to include actions to build the infrastructure for effective development work: donor database, donor identification, gift reports.
Maintain and manage a portfolio of high net worth/high interest prospects across the SEPI region by identifying gift opportunities that match their interests and personally cultivating, soliciting, and thanking these donors.
Create and clearly articulate a philanthropic case for SEPI needs, goals and objectives.
Track development results and report to appropriate SEPI staff and boards.

Key Qualifications

2-3 years' experience in fund raising, development or related fields preferred but not required.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to inspire and engage diverse audiences.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
Self-motivated, goal-oriented, and able to work independently as well as part of a team.
Passion for SEPl's mission and commitment to the Catholic faith and Hispanic Ministry.
Competitive salary and benefits package.
Opportunity to work in a mission-driven organization.
Professional development and growth opportunities.
Collaborative and inclusive work environment.

Application Process

Interested candidates should submit a resume and cover letter outlining their interest and qualifications for the Director of Development position to Dr. Olga Lucia Villar, aymeus@sepi.us. Please put the Job Title in the Subject Line. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.

SEPI is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applications from all qualified individuals committed to advancing Hispanic Ministry in the Southeast United States.

new_jobs.title: Director of Development, Alpha - $400,000/year USD
new_jobs.description:
Families at Alpha Miami rely on trusted advisors across every aspect of their lives. You will become the one they've been seeking in their child's education.

When uncertainty arises, when intuition signals something isn't quite right, or when a meaningful moment deserves to be shared with someone who truly understands—you'll be the person they reach out to. Your depth of knowledge about each family will enable you to foresee their needs before articulation becomes necessary, and you'll possess both the discernment and composure to address whatever they bring to you with confidentiality, genuine care, and seamless professionalism. Over time, your role will transcend being merely part of their child's educational institution. You'll become woven into the very fabric of how they navigate this chapter of family life.

The private school landscape in Miami is compact, deeply interconnected, and intolerant of errors. What happens at Alpha doesn't remain contained—it circulates through the networks that hold influence: residential buildings, exclusive clubs, yachts, charity galas, and the WhatsApp channels that determine where the next wave of families will apply. You'll grasp this ecosystem intuitively, drawing on a career spent in environments where reputation serves as the sole form of capital and where a single mistake can overshadow years of excellence. Your mission is to cultivate a community that families regard as precious, where membership feels natural and referrals emerge organically from the quality of the experience itself.

Miami's character is inherently international. Your work will involve Latin American families, global executives, households from finance and technology who have recently relocated to Miami, and internationally mobile families whose perspectives on education—and institutions more broadly—have been formed by experiences well beyond conventional American private schooling. Many navigate between Miami, São Paulo, Bogotá, Madrid, and New York with the same ease others show crossing neighborhoods, and they will evaluate Alpha against alternatives across the globe. The ideal candidate will be equally adept at managing cross-cultural family dynamics—and the expectation of sophisticated, fluid interpersonal relationships—as they are at navigating the more recognizable dimensions of Miami's private school community.

Alpha operates outside traditional educational frameworks. Students complete core academic requirements in two hours daily through AI-powered applications, dedicating remaining time to public speaking, critical thinking, and applied real-world projects. No conventional lectures. No unnecessary assignments. Performance outcomes in the top 1% nationally.

The families selecting Alpha have made a deliberate choice—and in a city where educational decisions carry social weight and everyone holds strong opinions, they will face constant questions. Your role is to ensure they never struggle for a response. If sustaining that conviction through years of sophisticated, internationally-informed scrutiny feels overwhelming, this position isn't suitable. If it represents the most compelling professional conversation available to you, continue reading.

What You Will Be Doing

Shape the cultural identity of the school community—establishing the tone, rituals, and implicit standards that foster genuine belonging—ensuring Alpha becomes a place families take pride in, not merely an institution they selected
Manage the complete parent relationship lifecycle—from initial contact through enrollment, continued engagement, and lasting advocacy—allowing faculty to concentrate on students while families maintain a reliable point of trust
Detect early signals when something feels misaligned, and address it with the discretion and judgment that preserves confidence—including the more difficult determination of when a family may not be an appropriate match
Maintain visibility in the spaces families occupy: morning arrival, afternoon departure, evening gatherings, and the spontaneous conversations that carry the most significance
Create a community that both domestic and international families regard as worthy of protection—and where word-of-mouth recommendations arise naturally from the caliber of experience provided
Communicate Alpha's narrative persuasively—in individual discussions, at formal events, and within the circles of discerning, globally-oriented families where the school's standing is genuinely established

What You Won’t Be Doing

Operating from behind a desk—your physical presence within the community defines this position
Maintaining conventional 9–5 schedules—early mornings, late afternoons, and evening commitments are integral to the role, not occasional requirements
Delegating challenging parent conversations to faculty or administrators—you assume complete ownership of these interactions
Relying on administrative infrastructure, established procedures, or a fully staffed team—you function with substantial autonomy and develop what's necessary
Approaching family relationships as accounts requiring management—this role rewards authentic connection rather than procedural adherence

Director Of Development Key Responsibilities

Serve as the primary trusted relationship at the heart of every family's journey at Alpha's Miami campus, and as the architect of its community culture. Maintain their confidence through difficult moments, establish the traditions and atmosphere that make belonging feel meaningful, and create the environment where satisfied families naturally become active advocates within the networks that influence Miami's private school ecosystem.

Basic Requirements

5+ years successfully earning and maintaining the confidence of high-net-worth or ultra-high-net-worth families in a relationship-intensive, high-touch capacity
Demonstrated success guiding sophisticated clients through consequential decisions—with measurable outcomes you can articulate: conversion rates, retention figures, referral generation
The discretion and poise necessary to navigate sensitive, confidential matters without creating lasting complications
Proven experience managing and developing personnel—providing coaching, maintaining elevated standards, and executing difficult staffing decisions—beyond simply managing relationships or client accounts
Inherent cross-cultural competence across both globally mobile families and established Miami households
AI-native orientation—you employ AI tools with fluency, understand their capabilities and constraints, and proactively identify superior solutions, even when you're not creating them independently
Miami-based, available for full-time on-site engagement, and adaptable to non-standard working hours
Legal authorization to work in the United States without requiring sponsorship

Nice-to-have Requirements

Background as a head of school, principal, superintendent, or in an equivalent educational leadership position
Professional-level fluency in Spanish or Portuguese
Personal or professional exposure to independent or non-traditional educational models
Pre-existing relationships within Miami's private school parent communities
Experience working with internationally mobile, Latin American, or globally connected family populations
A personal network extending through the neighborhoods, social clubs, and institutions where Alpha's families reside and make decisions

About Alpha

Join the team building school around student growth

Alpha reimagines the school day around faster learning, stronger mentorship, and more time for students to build real-world skills.

Roles with real student impact:
Alpha hires teachers (guides), operators, admissions experts, school & curriculum leaders, and remote team members.

Education work that pays like it matters:
Alpha offers six-figure roles for people ready to help build a radically better school experience.

A culture built for builders:
Alpha is fast-moving, ambitious, and student focused. It’s for people who want to improve the system, not maintain it.

Alpha is where you can do the most meaningful work of your career.

There is so much to cover for this exciting role, and space here is limited. Hit the Apply button if you found this interesting and want to learn more. We look forward to meeting you!

Working with us

This is a full-time (40 hours per week), long-term position. The position is immediately available and requires entering into an independent contractor agreement with Crossover as a Contractor of Record. The compensation level for this role is $200 USD/hour, which equates to $400,000 USD/year assuming 40 hours per week and 50 weeks per year. The payment period is weekly. Consult www.crossover.com/help-and-faqs for more details on this topic.

1. About Our Client:
The organization supports transformative Fellowships designed in partnership with leading corporations and foundations to aid entrepreneurs and leaders from underserved backgrounds. These Fellowships provide entrepreneurship and leadership training, mentorship, community stipends, long-term alumni support, and access to a global network of peers and opportunities. The Associate Director of Strategic Partnerships is responsible for developing and expanding a portfolio of corporate and foundation partnerships that support the organization''s Fellowships. This role focuses on creating and maintaining strategic collaborations that align business and community impact goals, ensuring the growth and sustainability of the Fellowship programs.

3. Responsibilities:
• Build and grow partnerships with corporations and foundations to support Fellowship programs.
• Create tailored and persuasive impact updates including decks, emails, and profiles for prospects and partners.
• Lead and prepare for prospect and partner meetings, including managing follow-ups and coordinating with internal teams.
• Map and engage complex stakeholder networks within partner organizations.
• Develop concept notes, decks, and grant applications demonstrating both business and community impact.
• Design points of connection that align partnerships with corporate strategy and goals across multiple teams.
• Coordinate closely with senior leadership to achieve partnership objectives.
• Collaborate with the Design Team on monitoring, evaluation, and learning to meet partner expectations.
• Lead special projects critical to partnership strategy, such as market landscape analyses and thought leadership development.

• Strong attention to detail with excellent organizational and project management skills.
• Proven ability to create compelling, well-designed communications and presentations.
• Experience managing complex stakeholder engagement.
• Strategic thinking with the ability to design and implement effective systems and processes.
• High integrity and sound judgment in handling sensitive information and decision-making.
• Results-driven with a proactive and persistent approach to responsibilities.

new_jobs.title: DIRECTOR OF DEVELOPMENT, WEST REGION
new_jobs.description:
Director of Development, West Region

Share

Full Time
New Orleans, LA
Posted 2 months ago

Website The National World War II Museum

The National WWII Museum is currently seeking a Director of Development for the West Region. The Director of Development for the West Region reports to the Vice President of Institutional Advancement and is responsible for securing financial resources to support the Museum’s campaign priorities in the areas of Education and Programs; Collections, Exhibits, and Campus; and Endowment and Planned Giving. The Director will build relationships with individual, corporate, and foundation donors and prospects in the West Region. The West Region is defined as California, Oregon, Washington, and Arizona.

The Museum’s fundraising program is national in scope, and Development Directors are assigned to regional responsibilities. The Director will join a major-gift-focused development team that includes Directors of Development for the following regions: East/MidAtlantic, South/Central, and Louisiana/Texas, as well as a Director of Planned Giving and a Director of Corporate and Foundations. In addition to reporting directly to the Vice President of Institutional Advancement, Directors of Development also report secondarily to the Assistant Vice President of Institutional Advancement, who oversees prospect and pipeline management.

Requirements

Identify, cultivate, and solicit leadership and major-gift prospects; recommend and implement strategies for cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, and engagement for prospects; work with executive and Museum leadership and board members as appropriate; and meet with prospects both in person and virtually, with regular travel throughout the assigned region.
Contribute to the development of capital, programmatic, and endowment fundraising strategies to increase giving, with an emphasis on expanding the individual major-gift effort.
Manage a portfolio of approximately 150 prospects through all fundraising cycles – identification, qualification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship – meeting monthly agreed-upon donor contact and proposal submission goals, tracking activity and contact, and maintaining updates in the Museum’s CRM, Raiser’s Edge.
Develop and implement solicitation plans for each prospect; craft compelling, donor-centric proposals to support them.
Coordinate stewardship and acknowledgment activities, ensuring regular, systematic contact with prospects across all communication channels.
Assist Museum administrators in identifying institutional funding needs and staying up to date on institutional priorities, programs, personnel, and events.
Serve as the principal liaison to major gift prospects for the Museum, developing and maintaining a collaborative work environment with Museum departments to ensure a seamless donor journey and engagement.
Ability to plan and schedule your travel when visiting major-gift prospects in your region.

Qualifications And Attributes

Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree and CFRE certification are a plus.
5+ years of direct fundraising experience – with a clear understanding of annual, major, capital, and deferred gifts – and a demonstrable track record of securing six- and seven-figure gifts.
Experience in higher education, healthcare, or large arts/cultural organizations; demonstrable interest in lifelong learning.
Mastery of the skills, principles, practices, and techniques associated with implementing and successfully executing major-gift work on a national scale.
Highly developed interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills.
Working knowledge of Blackbaud Raiser’s Edge.
Proactive strategic thinker with strong organizational skills and the ability to work both independently and as a team member.
Flexible, adaptable, and proactive team player, comfortable with ambiguity and able to navigate sophisticated donor relationships.
Ability to manage work responsibilities while building donor relationships that require regular travel, evening and weekend meetings, and time commitments.

Work Environment

This position can be based in New Orleans or on the West Coast. If West Coast-based, the position requires regional travel and quarterly travel to New Orleans. If New Orleans-based, travel to the West region is required twice monthly. This is a full-time position operating on Central Time business hours. Standard working hours are Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. The employee must maintain a reliable internet connection and a professional home workspace suitable for remote work.

In addition to offering competitive wages, the Museum’s benefits package includes:

Medical insurance – 2 plan options; Museum pays 75% of premium
Dental and vision insurance
Flexible spending account
401(k) – Museum matches 50% of employee contribution up to 6%; employer contribution full vested after 3 years of employment
Life insurance and AD&D – $15,000 policy employer paid; additional life and AD&D available
Long term disability insurance
Paid vacation and sick leave, 10 paid holidays per year
Free parking
Tuition assistance and professional development
Employee assistance program

The National WWII Museum is an equal opportunity employer and seeks diversity in its workforce. We are dedicated to a policy of non-discrimination in employment on any basis including race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. Consistent with the Americans with Disabilities Act, applicants may request accommodations needed to participate in the application process.

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
Director of Operations

Location:
On-Site

Compensation:
$130,000 Base Salary + Performance Incentive (On-Target Earnings $200,000+)
Industry:
Specialty Manufacturing | Off-Road Equipment

About the Opportunity
We are seeking a mission-driven Director of Operations to lead and scale a growing manufacturing business specializing in high-performance off-road equipment. This is a hands-on leadership role responsible for building high-performing teams, creating operational discipline, and driving execution across manufacturing, production, supply chain, quality, and distribution.

This opportunity is ideal for a military veteran who understands how to build teams, establish accountability, develop leaders, and execute under pressure. While manufacturing experience is important, we place an even greater emphasis on leadership, ownership, and the ability to create a culture built around performance and continuous improvement.
If you've successfully led people through complex environments, enjoy solving operational challenges, and want to leave an organization significantly better than you found it, we'd like to speak with you.

What You'll Do

Operational Leadership
Lead all day-to-day manufacturing and operational activities.
Drive productivity, efficiency, safety, and quality across the operation.
Develop scalable systems and standard operating procedures.
Identify operational bottlenecks and implement sustainable solutions.
Establish KPIs and accountability throughout the organization.

Team Development
Recruit, develop, and mentor supervisors and frontline leaders.
Build a culture centered on accountability, ownership, and continuous improvement.
Coach leaders through performance management and professional development.
Create succession plans and develop future operational leaders.
Foster strong communication between production, engineering, purchasing, and executive leadership.

Manufacturing Excellence
Improve throughput, reduce waste, and optimize production scheduling.
Partner with engineering to improve manufacturability and product quality.
Lead Lean Manufacturing and continuous improvement initiatives.
Ensure inventory, materials, and production resources are effectively managed.
Drive on-time delivery while maintaining quality standards.

Financial & Strategic Ownership
Own operational budgets and resource planning.
Improve gross margins through operational efficiencies.
Evaluate capital equipment and process improvement opportunities.
Support strategic growth initiatives and capacity planning.
Present operational performance metrics and improvement plans to executive leadership.

Safety & Compliance
Build and reinforce a world-class safety culture.
Ensure compliance with OSHA and all applicable manufacturing regulations.
Maintain quality systems and operational documentation.

What We're Looking For

The ideal candidate is a builder—not simply a manager.

You know how to earn trust, develop people, create structure where none exists, and hold teams accountable while maintaining high morale. You thrive in environments that require decisive leadership, operational discipline, and continuous improvement.
Military leadership experience is strongly preferred, particularly candidates who have led teams in demanding, high-performance environments.

Qualifications
Military leadership experience strongly preferred.
8+ years of operations leadership experience in manufacturing, industrial, or related environments.
Experience leading managers, supervisors, and cross-functional teams.
Demonstrated success improving operational performance and building scalable systems.
Strong understanding of manufacturing operations, production planning, quality, and supply chain.
Experience managing budgets and operational KPIs.
Lean Manufacturing, Six Sigma, or continuous improvement experience preferred.
Excellent communication and leadership skills.
Bachelor's degree preferred; equivalent leadership experience will be considered.

Success in the First 12 Months

The successful Director of Operations will:
Build a highly engaged and accountable leadership team.
Improve production efficiency and operational throughput.
Implement standardized processes across the operation.
Strengthen safety, quality, and operational metrics.
Improve delivery performance and customer satisfaction.
Develop future leaders while building a culture of ownership and continuous improvement.
Position the operation for sustained, profitable growth.

Compensation & Benefits
Base Salary:
$130,000
Performance Bonus:
Significant incentive opportunity
On-Target Earnings:
$200,000+
Comprehensive health benefits
Paid Time Off
Retirement savings program
Professional development opportunities
Opportunity to make a lasting impact within a growing specialty manufacturing company.

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
Major Areas of Focus

The Director of Operations (DOO) has P&L responsibility for their business unit. They are directly responsible for all sales in their region and the employees, equipment, real estate, and general infrastructure required for the fulfillment of those sales. The DOO works closely with the Director of Home Office and Director of Business Development.

The DOO has the most employees under his/her care and has a great responsibility to ensure all are developed according to ECF Culture. Our organizational plan places the DOO only four levels away from the front line. He knows the appearance of his front-line, their names, ranks, strengths, weaknesses, goals, and dreams. He is a steward of the company's mission statement.

The DOO is responsible for the development and implementation of the operating budget. This includes the sales budget. The DOO oversees Account Executives and supports the sales process by promoting the ECF Brand, attending sales presentations, and negotiating agreements.

Accountable for the management of a division of the company that spans multiple geographic markets
Is a sales, operations, and safety leader
Is a champion of ECF Culture, and gives thoughtful attention and care to front-line workers
Works closely with Executive Management
Reports to the President

Essential duties and responsibilities include the following. Other duties may also be assigned.

Safety

Always sets the example in matters of safety
Responsible for implementation of all training schedules and initiatives as set by the Corporate Safety Committee
Directly responsible for any lost time accidents that occur within the division coverage area
Directly responsible for safety infractions committed by division personnel
Maintains self-certifications including OSHA 10, ASM (if division spans snow markets), and appropriate pesticide licenses

Training & Development

Responsible for implementation of weekly Crew Leader muster training as scheduled by the Home Office
Ensures that operations managers and supervisors are training crew leaders and crew members in a meaningful way including training on soft skills, technical skills, and safety.

Leadership

Cares about people, and helps them to grow and achieve their personal goals
Maintains a visible and strong presence at the Service Centers, satellites of the division, and on the job sites
Is a professional steward of company initiatives, policies, and priorities as set forth by ownership
Always preserves and promotes the Chain of Command
Ensures that all subordinates are closely adhering to company policies
Participates in and drives the ECF Egress Standard and the ECF Ingress Standard
Develops and mentors personnel by ensuring that APR and MPR performance review programs are on schedule and effectively implemented

Financial

Builds draft annual operating budgets with the company Controller and submits them for approval
Builds monthly live budgets with the company Controller and submits them for approval
Manages the division’s P&L, and is directly responsible for all cost of goods sold expenses.
Manages AR for the division
Responsible for purchasing goods, services, and materials in line with job budget; a supervisor must approve overages
Manages all inventory of the division
Completes all appropriate internal documentation related to purchasing procedures
Manages and drives job budget of hours and materials, ensures margin budget is met

Facilities

Is directly responsible for all facilities and fleet belonging to the division
Ensures that the facilities and fleet are clean and orderly; within ECF 5S standards
Manages and directs fleet maintenance and mechanical service operation

Client Management

Coordinates with team members, clients, and operations leaders to ensure customer satisfaction is at its highest possible level, before, during, and after the delivery of a completed project scope
Manages customer service-related activities
Works closely with the Director of Business Development to ensure that handoffs of new clients are seamless
Is directly responsible for local client retention from a fulfillment perspective

Human Resources

Follows company policies and ensures that team members are obeying all safety, quality, and excellence policies and guidelines
Drives recruiting, the hiring process, and development of team members
Oversees all hiring managers (Production Managers) and ensures their departments are always staffed
Manages job hours and ensures that adequate staff is available to meet the needs of assigned projects
Follows and administers appropriate disciplinary protocols that are applicable to subordinates
Ensures that all branding and uniform compliance are always in order
Works closely with corporate human resources

Sales

Ensure all company policies and systems are in full use
Ensures that production managers are focused on providing proposals to clients for any assigned previously sold and established service contracts
Conduct or participate in bidding & takeoff processes, for any projects assigned by the sales team or the corporate office
Seek and obtain referrals for additional work opportunities

Quality Control

Ensure every project meets quality standards upon completion
Execute a Quality Control Program on assigned projects
Obtain, review and act on quality control inspection results
Initiates and manages annual customer experience surveys

Image Control

Ensure assigned fleet, personnel uniforms, grooming, facility cleanliness, and job sites meet our corporate standards of professional image
Conduct etiquette, behavior, and professional standards training to all subordinates
Ensure that all personnel are observing safety standards during working hours, at job sites, in public places, in transit, and at branch facilities, and such standards are clearly seen by the general public

Language Skills

While performing duties, employees are regularly required to use written and oral communication skills; may read and interpret data, information, and documents; observe and respond to people and situations; learn and apply new information or skills; work under deadlines; and interact with others encountered in the course of work. Proficiency in English is required, the ability to speak fluent Spanish is highly beneficial.

Physical Demands

The employee frequently is required to stand, walk and sit. The employee is occasionally required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel; reach with hands and arms; climb or balance; and stoop, kneel, or crouch. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 60 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Long working hours are required for emergency, urgent response or winter weather storm management operations.

Work Environment

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is occasionally exposed to wet and/or humid conditions, moving mechanical parts, fumes or airborne particles, toxic or caustic chemicals, and outside weather conditions. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate.

Travel & Development

Travel throughout the market served by the Allentown Service Center, from time to time for purposes of training, the employee may be required to travel to other offices or training centers operated by East Coast Facilities, Inc.

Security

The employee may not use illicit drugs during their employment with ECF. ECF operates in several high-security environments which require individuals who are able to pass security background checks. The employee is required to pass security background checks that meet our underwriting standards. The employee is required to have a valid driver's license and driving record that meets our underwriting standards. We reserve the right to reject an applicant or terminate an employee whose criminal or driving record fails to meet our underwriting criteria.

The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by people assigned to this job family chart. They are not intended to be a comprehensive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required of employees in this job family series. Other duties may be assigned as needed.

new_jobs.title: CA Solar and Energy Storage Policy Director
new_jobs.description:
The primary goal of the Policy Director is to develop a policy roadmap for the growth of customer-sited solar and storage in California, and to implement it with CALSSA’s policy team and members.

The Policy Director reports directly to the Executive Director, working closely with the Board of Directors and the entire CALSSA team.

Position Responsibilities

Advocacy & Policy Development

Develop policy goals and determine positions
Work directly with Executive Director to craft policy goals and long term vision
Coordinate with staff and member companies to develop CALSSA positions
Support legislative initiatives
Research, analyze and track bills
Recommend policy details for sponsored legislation
Provide policy analysis for legislative staff and at committee hearings
Represent CALSSA at state agencies
Help track relevant CPUC, CEC and CAISO activity related to distributed solar and energy storage
Coordinate with staff and member companies to develop CALSSA positions
Compose filings and attend stakeholder meetings
Meet with Commissioners and staff
Oversee policy staff
Together with the executive director, help CALSSA policy staff succeed in their work
Meet with staff regularly on problem solving and advocacy strategy
Edit filings written by other staff
Assist in policy research in response to member inquiries
Interact with CALSSA members
Facilitate input from members on CALSSA decision making through managing committee meetings and direct communications
Explain details of programs and tariffs to member companies in direct communications and by producing webinars and other training events
Produce factsheets and website updates
Attend regional meetings

Building Public Support

Translate and communicate the importance of legislative and regulatory decisions to CALSSA membership, elected officials, media, and the public through press releases, fact sheets, emails, social media posts, and opinion pieces
Engage allies and work with coalition partners
Speak at conferences and public meetings

Organizational Development

Work with membership staff to recruit member companies
Help with fundraising efforts to support non-profit association
Attend team meetings, board meetings, and strategy development retreats

Experience

A strong candidate will have experience with policy advocacy and familiarity with issues facing customer-sited solar energy and energy storage. They will have excellent communication skills, a demonstrated ability to work as part of a team, and a feel for the regulatory and legislative processes. Specific solar experience is a plus but not a prerequisite.

ABOUT CALSSA

The California Solar & Storage Association (CALSSA) has advanced the common interest of the solar and storage industry for over 45 years, making California the most robust market in the U.S. The association is the state’s largest clean energy business group with over 550 member companies representing an array of businesses that manufacture, design, install, finance, and provide other resources to the growing local solar thermal, photovoltaic, and storage markets in California.

new_jobs.title: Program Director - Data Strategy, Policy & Advocacy
new_jobs.description:
At ACT, Your Work Makes a Difference

Education has power – a power that changes lives forever. It creates opportunities that lift up individuals, their families, and sparks societal change that echoes through generations to come. From our grassroots we have fought the good fight for equity in education, and we remain devoted to helping anyone who struggles to access that power. This is what matters to us and we must do better -- we’ve never been more sure of our purpose.

ACT team members are part of an organization dedicated to a mission that has never been more important: Helping people achieve education and workplace success. Advancing that mission within our organization, by helping our team members achieve education and workplace success of their own, is core to our values. ACT values a diverse workplace and is committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable environment in which every team member has an opportunity to grow.

We want our team members to have the well-being and confidence they need to do their best work, in an environment where both they and ACT thrive. To support this, our total rewards include company paid life insurance, medical, dental, vision, flexible health and dependent spending accounts, 401(k) retirement savings with company match, paid holidays, paid time off, and so much more. You can find a comprehensive list of our benefits here.

We are seeking a Program Director, Data Strategy, Policy & Advocacy
to help us fulfill that mission. As education systems face growing demands for data-informed decision-making, from assessment design to college readiness benchmarks, ACT is building its internal capacity to generate, synthesize, and communicate rigorous analyses that advance equitable student outcomes.

The Program Director, Data Strategy, Policy & Advocacy
will play a central role in building and sustaining this capacity. This is an opportunity for a mission-driven data professional who thrives at the intersection of analytics, policy research, and strategic communication – someone who can translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for policymakers, partners, and the public.

The Program Director, Data Strategy, Policy & Advocacy will lead high-impact work across three areas:

Data storytelling and thought leadership to support ACT’s influence in the field
Policy research and analysis to inform ACT’s external positions and priorities
Partner Engagement and evidence building to grow ACT’s evidence base and assess the impact of ACT programs and partnerships

The finalist will be hired at the salary and level commensurate with their qualifications. For this position, we anticipate offering an annual salary in the range of $115,000 to $130,000.
ACT manages salaries within the range based on relevant factors including, skills, experience, and internal equity. The position is incentive eligible.

Location:
This is a remote position, based in the United States.

Visa:
Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. without the need for visa sponsorship.

What You Will Be Working On

Data Storytelling and Thought Leadership: Embed data-driven decision-making across ACT’s Government and Public Relations team and serve as a strategic thought partner to ACT leadership and customers.
Key responsibilities include:
Support leadership in using data to inform strategic planning and resource allocation. Design and maintain data tools, dashboards, presentations, and visualizations that make key metrics accessible and actionable for internal and external stakeholders
Proactively identify research questions and analytic opportunities aligned with ACT’s policy priorities
Create feedback loops between research findings, program design, and partnership strategy
Provide capacity-building support to colleagues: training staff on data tools, analytic frameworks, and evidence-based practices
Maintain a pulse on external data trends, policy developments, and methodological advances relevant to ACT’s work

Policy Research & Analysis: Generate rigorous, policy-relevant analyses that inform ACT’s positions and contribute to national conversations about education equity, assessment, and college and career readiness.
Key responsibilities include:
Provide strategic input to inform quantitative and mixed-methods research studies to answer high-priority policy questions
Conduct literature reviews and synthesize research on education policy topics, presenting findings in actionable formats for policy and program staff
Analyze large-scale education datasets (assessment results, demographic trends, program outcomes) to identify patterns and inform strategic recommendations
Develop and operationalize metrics and frameworks for evaluating policy-relevant outcomes
Incorporate equity analysis to understand disparities in outcomes across student populations
Identify relevant external data sources (federal, state, institutional) and build efficient workflows for data acquisition and management
Translate complex analyses into clear written products (briefs, memos, reports) for non-technical audiences including policymakers and funders

Partner Engagement & Evidence Building: Design and implement data-sharing partnerships that build an evidence base for ACT programs, partnerships, and policy initiatives.
Key responsibilities include:
Systematize data-sharing agreements and data collection workflows with external partners (districts, states, institutions)
Support the development of reporting templates that can scale across partner engagements
Analyze variation in stakeholder contexts and identify conditions associated with strong outcomes
Support the development of clear metrics that define and operationalize success for ACT programs and partnerships
Generate impact reports that translate research findings into actionable insights for internal teams and external stakeholders

This could be the job for you if you have (minimum requirements):

At least 7 years of experience using data and analysis at the district or state level to inform decision-making in education, government, nonprofit, or social impact contexts, including demonstrated experience working with education data, advising senior leaders and shaping organizational strategy
Strong quantitative analysis skills
Familiarity with educational research designs and methods
Experience producing external-facing policy reports, case studies, or thought leadership
Experience building dashboards and other data visualizations, e.g. Tableau, MS Power BI or similar software
Experience designing surveys, analyzing assessment data, or evaluating programs or policies
Ability to translate complex data and analyses into clear written reports and presentations for non-technical audiences
Proven project management skills and ability to independently lead and manage multiple complex workstreams simultaneously, with minimal oversight
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Demonstrated commitment to improving educational outcomes and advancing equity
Ability to Travel approximately 10-15%
Bachelor's degree in education policy, public policy, economics, statistics, data science, or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

It’s a Plus If You Have

Experience at an SEA, large LEA, or federal agency working with K–12 or postsecondary education data, including state assessment or accountability data
Experience partnering with school districts, state education agencies, or higher education institutions
Strong stakeholder management skills and experience working cross-functionally
Curiosity, learning orientation, and comfort working in a mission-driven, fast-paced environment
Master’s degree in education policy, public policy, economics, statistics, data science, or a related field

About ACT

When ACT was founded in 1959, it disrupted the assessment industry with a new approach to helping students better understand their readiness so they could take steps to improve it. By leveraging our expertise and authority in assessment and research, we will again disrupt the industry—helping more people learn, better measure their progress, and improve their navigation through life’s transitions.

new_jobs.description:

W2 Employee
$200K annually

Families who select Alpha already rely on trusted advisors across every dimension of their lives. This role fills the one advisory relationship they've been lacking.

You will become the person they reach out to when uncertainty arises, when something doesn't feel right, or when they want to share a milestone they know you will appreciate. You'll develop familiarity with each family that allows you to foresee their needs before they articulate them—and you'll possess the judgment and composure to address whatever they bring with discretion, warmth, and ease. Over time, you won't simply represent their child's school. You'll become woven into the fabric of how they navigate this stage of their family's journey.

This level of trust is not built through systems. It emerges from presence—regular, sustained, and unrushed. You'll be accessible during morning arrival and afternoon departure, at significant evening gatherings, and in spontaneous one-on-one exchanges that rarely appear on schedules. You'll cultivate a community that families feel invested in protecting, where belonging is natural and referrals flow from the quality of the experience itself.

Alpha operates differently from conventional schools. Students complete core academics in two hours daily through AI-enabled applications, then dedicate remaining time to public speaking, critical thinking, and applied projects. No traditional lectures. No unnecessary assignments. Performance in the top 1% nationally. The families who enroll are investing in something they value deeply—but confidence requires ongoing reinforcement.

You'll sustain that dialogue with every family, through every question, for the duration of their enrollment. If the prospect of championing an unconventional approach to discerning skeptics feels daunting, this position is not suitable. If it represents the most compelling work you could pursue, continue reading.

What You Will Be Doing

Take ownership of the relationship with every family at your campus—the evolving, deepening connection that ensures parents feel recognized, not merely attended to
Identify potential concerns in advance and address them with the discretion and interpersonal skill that preserves confidence
Maintain visible presence in family spaces: morning arrival (7–9am), afternoon departure (2–4pm), evening parent gatherings, and select weekend programming
Develop a campus community with authentic depth—events, rituals, and touchpoints that foster a sense of belonging to something families consider worth safeguarding
Nurture parent champions organically, designing experiences of such consistent quality that referrals emerge naturally
Sustain the dialogue about Alpha's AI-driven model with families managing screen time questions, skeptical relatives, and periodic uncertainty—not only during enrollment but throughout their campus experience
Recognize when a family is not aligned and manage that reality with the same care you apply to all interactions—community protection is integral to service
Create the structures and routines this role demands in a setting where established protocols are still emerging

What You Won’t Be Doing

Overseeing enrollment processes—that falls under the Admissions Director; your responsibility begins after families enroll and intensifies over time
Operating from an office—your community presence defines the role
Maintaining standard 9–5 schedules—early mornings, late afternoons, and evening programming are routine components, not occasional requirements
Relying on administrative infrastructure, standardized procedures, or a complete team—you function with significant independence and create what is necessary
Managing family relationships as transactional accounts—this role values authentic engagement over procedural adherence

Director Of Partnerships And Community Outreach Key Responsibilities

Serve as the central trusted relationship in every family's Alpha experience. Maintain their confidence during challenging periods, cultivate a community where belonging feels meaningful, and establish conditions under which satisfied families naturally become outspoken advocates.

Basic Requirements

Currently living within commuting range of one of the following campuses OR prepared to relocate within 60 days (preference given to candidates with established local community ties): Denver, CO; Greenwich, CT; Boca Raton, FL; Tampa, FL; Boston, MA; Bethesda, MD; Charlotte, NC; Las Vegas, NV; Oklahoma City, OK; Tulsa, OK; Nashville, TN; Austin, TX; Dallas (Carrollton), TX; Fort Worth, TX; Houston (The Woodlands), TX; Park City, UT; Burlington, VT; Kirkland, WA; or Jackson Hole, WY
Authorized to work legally in the United States without sponsorship requirements
5+ years in relationship-focused roles serving high-net-worth or ultra-high-net-worth families—private school admissions, luxury hospitality, philanthropic development, or any context where discretion, warmth, and social acuity were equally critical to competence
Proven capacity to preserve relationships through complex situations with sophisticated families and maintain trust throughout
Authentic presence and communication approach that builds confidence rapidly with affluent, high-expectation families—genuine rather than rehearsed
Direct familiarity with independent, private, or alternative education—through professional work or parenting—that provides credible standing when families challenge the model
Authentic conviction in AI-enabled education and capacity to sustain that belief genuinely across years of parent engagement, not merely during initial enrollment
Flexibility for variable hours: early mornings (7–9am), late afternoons (2–4pm), evening events 2–3 times monthly, and occasional weekend activities

Nice-to-have Requirements

Exceptional candidates will possess at least one of these qualities:

Established connections within the local affluent community providing immediate, organic credibility on campus
Private school or independent school background with demonstrated success in family retention and community building—beyond enrollment metrics alone
Experience managing high-stakes family dynamics where your discretion and judgment were instrumental in preserving trust
Natural ability to design experiences—gatherings, moments, settings—where participants feel connected to something they value protecting
Prior startup or early-stage organizational experience where you built from the ground up and maintained composure amid rapid change

About Alpha

Join the team building school around student growth

Alpha reimagines the school day around faster learning, stronger mentorship, and more time for students to build real-world skills.

Roles with real student impact:
Alpha hires teachers (guides), operators, admissions experts, school & curriculum leaders, and remote team members.

Education work that pays like it matters:
Alpha offers six-figure roles for people ready to help build a radically better school experience.

A culture built for builders:
Alpha is fast-moving, ambitious, and student focused. It’s for people who want to improve the system, not maintain it.

Alpha is where you can do the most meaningful work of your career.

new_jobs.description:
1. About Our Client:
The AI Access Initiative (2AI) operates within the artificial intelligence sector, focusing on leveraging AI innovations to address poverty in low- and middle-income countries. The organization aims to bridge the development gap by scaling AI-enabled programs that benefit tens or hundreds of millions of people living in poverty. It collaborates with global development actors, AI labs, and researchers to create open-sourced resources and implement impactful AI solutions in agriculture and health, with outreach already impacting millions in countries such as India.

2. About the Opportunity:
The Chief Program Officer will lead and scale programmatic efforts across agriculture and health portfolios, playing a critical role in expanding AI-enabled interventions to reach tens of millions of beneficiaries. This position involves setting program strategy, building and managing teams, and establishing systems to ensure effective program delivery and learning. As a key leadership team member, the Chief Program Officer influences the organization’s direction and represents it to partners and funders.

3. Responsibilities:
• Serve on the executive leadership team, shaping strategy, culture, and priorities.
• Hire, mentor, and develop leadership and program staff.
• Establish rigorous learning architectures with clear hypotheses, measurement, iteration, and reporting.
• Guide resource allocation based on cost-effectiveness evidence.
• Develop and maintain open-sourced playbooks and insights.
• Set and execute ambitious program strategies and expansion plans.
• Collaborate closely with regional and program leaders.
• Build program teams, systems, and partnerships to support scaling.
• Engage with funders and external stakeholders as a key organizational representative.

4. Requirements:
• Experience leading large-scale program operations, preferably across multiple geographies.
• Strong ability to recruit and develop high-performing teams.
• Expertise in designing program strategy and organizational infrastructure.
• Skills in implementing learning and measurement processes.
• Ability to work collaboratively with diverse stakeholders including governments and partners.
• Experience in the global development, AI, health, or agriculture sectors is advantageous.
